export const meta = {
  name: 'glamdring-riposte-rampage-impl',
  description: 'Implement the Riposte & Rampage combat-depth + juice package across Glamdring, one agent per disjoint file set against a frozen spec',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Implement', detail: 'parallel file-owner agents implement their file(s) against the shared spec + public contract' },
  ],
}

const REPO = 'C:\\Users\\duran\\Documents\\godot\\glamdring'

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// FROZEN SPEC. No backticks anywhere. Code shown with indentation. Every cross-
// file name/signature is fixed by the PUBLIC CONTRACT section so parallel
// file-owner agents implement against identical interfaces.
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
const SPEC = `
GLAMDRING — "RIPOSTE & RAMPAGE" COMBAT-DEPTH + JUICE UPDATE
Game: 1v1 reaction combat, Godot 4.6, fixed 16 ticks/sec. Player invincible (v1),
goal = enemy hp to 0. All gameplay logic runs in fight.gd _on_tick(). Tunables in
balance.json via the Balance autoload. Sprites are single-cell-per-stance, swapped
via region_rect; NO animation. New visual states REUSE existing cells + a modulate
tint. Comments may mix Czech + English to match existing style.

================ THE 5 FEATURES (what we are adding) ================

F1. PERFECT PARRY -> STAGGER + RIPOSTE
  - Active parry (player blocking a WEAK enemy swing within the first
    block_active_ticks of holding block) ALREADY drains enemy fatigue. NOW it also
    ARMS A RIPOSTE on the player: player.set_riposte_ready() sets a window.
  - PERFECT PARRY = the block was started very recently (blocking_ticks_elapsed <=
    perfect_parry_ticks) at the moment the weak swing resolves. A perfect parry
    additionally STAGGERS the enemy: enemy.enter_stagger() -> new "staggered" state
    (wide-open, cannot act, all hits land), for stagger_ticks.
  - RIPOSTE: while the player's riposte window is active (riposte_ready_ticks > 0),
    the player's NEXT attack that actually DEALS DAMAGE is multiplied by
    riposte_damage_multiplier, then the riposte is consumed. A whiff (hitting an
    idle/threat enemy = no damage) does NOT consume the riposte.
  - Perfect parry is a strict subset of active parry (perfect_parry_ticks <
    block_active_ticks). Passive block (held too long) still just protects: no
    drain, no riposte (unchanged).

F2. HIT-STOP (freeze frames) -- pure presentation, gameplay-neutral
  - On impactful events, fight.gd freezes the tick loop for a few real seconds while
    still updating shake + particles + redraw. Durations (seconds, floats):
    hitstop_weak (small), hitstop_strong (big), hitstop_parry, hitstop_stagger.
  - Weak player hit -> hitstop_weak. Strong hit -> hitstop_strong. Riposte OR execute
    hit -> hitstop_strong (the bigger one). Active parry -> hitstop_parry. Perfect
    parry/stagger -> hitstop_stagger. Player getting hit -> hitstop_weak.

F3. COMBO + RANK -- feedback layer, no rule change
  - player.combo increments on every player attack that deals damage to the enemy.
    Resets to 0 when the player gets hit (enemy swing lands) OR when combo_timeout_ticks
    pass with no new damaging hit. player.max_combo tracks the session best.
  - Rank from combo: >=15 S, >=10 A, >=5 B, >=1 C, else "" (none).
  - HUD shows current combo + rank at top-center while combo >= 1, and max combo +
    rank on the win overlay.

F4. ENEMY FEINTS -- AI mind-game
  - When the enemy decides a WEAK attack, with probability feint_probability it is a
    FEINT: it telegraphs threat_weak identically to a real attack, but at the swing
    tick it does NOT swing -- instead it snaps to a short idle (feint_idle_ticks) and
    then decides again. Baits premature block/dodge. Strong attacks never feint.

F5. READABILITY TINTS + EXECUTE
  - Threat onset pulse: entering threat_weak/threat_strong sets threat_pulse_ticks
    (=THREAT_PULSE_TICKS const) so the sprite flashes bright yellow for ~2 ticks.
  - staggered state tint: cyan wash (reuses exhausted cell index 5).
  - Execute: when enemy.hp <= execute_threshold_hp, the enemy sprite gets a steady
    reddish tint, and player hits that land deal execute_damage_multiplier bonus
    damage (stacks multiplicatively with riposte; round at the end).
  - Player riposte-ready tint: while riposte_ready_ticks > 0 the player sprite gets a
    golden brightened tint.
  - Modulate priority (highest first): hit_flash > (player: riposte-ready) /
    (enemy: staggered) > threat pulse > execute > WHITE.

================ NEW balance.json KEYS + balance.gd ================

Add to balance.json (keep existing keys; add these into the named sections; add a new
"fx" section). Values below are the defaults to ship:

  "player": { ...,
    "riposte_window_ticks": 14,
    "riposte_damage_multiplier": 1.6,
    "perfect_parry_ticks": 3 },
  "enemy_basic": { ...,
    "stagger_ticks": 16,
    "feint_probability": 0.15,
    "feint_idle_ticks": 6 },
  "fight": { ...,
    "execute_threshold_hp": 25,
    "execute_damage_multiplier": 1.5,
    "combo_timeout_ticks": 48 },
  "fx": {
    "hitstop_weak": 0.04,
    "hitstop_strong": 0.10,
    "hitstop_parry": 0.08,
    "hitstop_stagger": 0.14 }

balance.gd: declare matching vars with these defaults, and parse them in _load_balance().
  Player (int unless noted):
    var riposte_window_ticks: int = 14
    var riposte_damage_multiplier: float = 1.6   (float! parse with float(...))
    var perfect_parry_ticks: int = 3
  Enemy basic:
    var stagger_ticks: int = 16
    var feint_probability: float = 0.15          (float!)
    var feint_idle_ticks: int = 6
  Fight:
    var execute_threshold_hp: int = 25
    var execute_damage_multiplier: float = 1.5   (float!)
    var combo_timeout_ticks: int = 48
  FX (new section "fx", all float seconds):
    var hitstop_weak: float = 0.04
    var hitstop_strong: float = 0.10
    var hitstop_parry: float = 0.08
    var hitstop_stagger: float = 0.14
  In _load_balance, after the existing fight block, add:
    var fx: Dictionary = data.get("fx", {}) as Dictionary
    hitstop_weak = float(fx.get("hitstop_weak", hitstop_weak))   etc.
  And add the new player/enemy_basic/fight gets alongside the existing ones in their
  blocks (use int(...) for ints, float(...) for the three multipliers/probability).

================ PUBLIC CONTRACT (exact names/signatures — DO NOT DEVIATE) ================

player.gd adds:
  var riposte_ready_ticks: int = 0
  var combo: int = 0
  var combo_timer_ticks: int = 0
  var max_combo: int = 0
  func set_riposte_ready() -> void          # riposte_ready_ticks = Balance.riposte_window_ticks; _update_sprite()
  func is_riposte_ready() -> bool           # return riposte_ready_ticks > 0
  func consume_riposte() -> void            # riposte_ready_ticks = 0; _update_sprite()
  func is_perfect_parry() -> bool           # return state == &"blocking" and blocking_ticks_elapsed <= Balance.perfect_parry_ticks
  func add_combo() -> void                  # combo += 1; max_combo = maxi(max_combo, combo); combo_timer_ticks = Balance.combo_timeout_ticks
  func reset_combo() -> void                # combo = 0; combo_timer_ticks = 0
  func combo_rank() -> StringName           # >=15 &"S", >=10 &"A", >=5 &"B", >=1 &"C", else &""

enemy.gd adds:
  func enter_stagger() -> void              # is_feint = false; set_state(&"staggered", _stagger_ticks())
  state &"staggered" handled in _advance_state_after_expiry -> _enter_idle()
  &"staggered" added to SPRITE_INDEX mapping to cell index 5 (reuse exhausted cell)
  tunable accessors (base defaults): _stagger_ticks()->int (12), _feint_probability()->float (0.0), _feint_idle_ticks()->int (6)

enemy_basic.gd adds overrides:
  func _stagger_ticks() -> int: return Balance.stagger_ticks
  func _feint_probability() -> float: return Balance.feint_probability
  func _feint_idle_ticks() -> int: return Balance.feint_idle_ticks

hud.gd update() — NEW exact signature (3 params appended):
  func update(p_enemy_hp: int, p_enemy_fatigue: int, p_enemy_state: StringName,
      p_enemy_state_ticks: int, p_player_state: StringName, p_player_state_ticks: int,
      p_dodge_cd: int, p_show_win: bool, p_combo: int, p_combo_rank: StringName,
      p_max_combo: int) -> void

fight.gd calls (must match the above exactly):
  hud.update(enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
      player.state, p_ticks, player.dodge_cooldown_ticks, game_over,
      player.combo, player.combo_rank(), player.max_combo)
  And: player.set_riposte_ready(), player.is_perfect_parry(), enemy.enter_stagger(),
  player.add_combo(), player.reset_combo(), player.is_riposte_ready(), player.consume_riposte()

================ PER-FILE IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL ================

--- player.gd ---
  - Add the vars + methods from the contract.
  - In tick(): after the existing cooldown decrements, also decrement riposte_ready_ticks
    if > 0 (call _update_sprite when it hits 0 is optional; it is refreshed at end of tick
    anyway). Decrement combo timer: if combo_timer_ticks > 0: combo_timer_ticks -= 1; if it
    reaches 0: combo = 0. (combo bar will refresh via HUD each tick from fight.gd.)
  - _current_visual_stance() unchanged.
  - _update_sprite(): change the modulate selection to priority:
        if hit_flash_ticks > 0: mod = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE
        elif riposte_ready_ticks > 0: mod = SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE
        else: mod = Color.WHITE
    Add const SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE := Color(1.6, 1.35, 0.4, 1.0)
  - Do NOT change input handling, attack/dodge logic, or existing consts otherwise.

--- enemy.gd + enemy_basic.gd ---
  enemy.gd:
  - SPRITE_INDEX: add &"staggered": 5 (reuse exhausted cell).
  - Add consts: THREAT_PULSE_TICKS := 2,
      STAGGER_MODULATE := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0),
      THREAT_PULSE_MODULATE := Color(1.7, 1.5, 0.5, 1.0),
      EXECUTE_MODULATE := Color(1.35, 0.55, 0.55, 1.0)
  - Add vars: var is_feint: bool = false ; var threat_pulse_ticks: int = 0
  - tick(): at the very start (after swing_this_tick = false), decrement threat_pulse_ticks
    if > 0. Keep the rest. NOTE the defensive branch at line ~94
        elif (state == &"threat_weak" or state == &"threat_strong") and state_ticks_remaining == 0:
            swing_this_tick = true
    MUST be guarded so feints never swing: change condition to also require "and not is_feint".
  - enter_stagger() per contract.
  - _decide_attack(): after pending_attack_kind is chosen, roll feint:
        is_feint = (pending_attack_kind == &"weak" and rng.randf() < _feint_probability())
    Then set the threat state as today AND set threat_pulse_ticks = THREAT_PULSE_TICKS.
    (Strong path also sets threat_pulse_ticks.)
  - _advance_state_after_expiry():
      &"threat_weak":
          if is_feint:
              is_feint = false
              set_state(&"idle", _feint_idle_ticks())   # short feint recovery idle; re-decides on expiry
          else:
              swing_this_tick = true
              set_state(&"recovery_weak", _recovery_weak_ticks())
      &"threat_strong": unchanged (swing_this_tick = true; recovery_strong)
      add &"staggered": _enter_idle()
      keep existing idle/recovery/exhausted cases.
  - _enter_idle(): also set is_feint = false (safety) in addition to existing body.
  - Add base tunable accessors _stagger_ticks/_feint_probability/_feint_idle_ticks with the
    base defaults from the contract.
  - _update_sprite(): keep computing idx from SPRITE_INDEX.get(state, 0). Change modulate
    selection to priority:
        if hit_flash_ticks > 0: mod = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE
        elif state == &"staggered": mod = STAGGER_MODULATE
        elif threat_pulse_ticks > 0: mod = THREAT_PULSE_MODULATE
        elif hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp: mod = EXECUTE_MODULATE
        else: mod = Color.WHITE
    Apply mod to both sprite and weapon_sprite as today.
  enemy_basic.gd:
  - Add the three overrides from the contract.

--- fight.gd ---
  - Add var hitstop_timer: float = 0.0
  - Add consts: COLOR_STAGGER_FX := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
                COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX := Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
  - _process(delta): replace the tick pump so hitstop freezes ticks:
        func _process(delta: float) -> void:
            if hitstop_timer > 0.0:
                hitstop_timer = maxf(0.0, hitstop_timer - delta)
            else:
                tick_acc += delta
                while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION and hitstop_timer <= 0.0:
                    tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
                    _on_tick()
            _update_shake(delta)
            _update_particles(delta)
            queue_redraw()
    (A hit inside _on_tick sets hitstop_timer > 0, which breaks the while loop and freezes
    the next ticks; tick_acc is not advanced during the freeze.)
  - Add helper: func _trigger_hitstop(amount: float) -> void: hitstop_timer = maxf(hitstop_timer, amount)
  - _resolve_enemy_swing(): keep the dodge-miss branch. Replace the weak+blocking branch:
        if kind == &"weak" and player.state == &"blocking":
            if player.is_active_block():
                enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
                player.set_riposte_ready()
                if player.is_perfect_parry():
                    enemy.enter_stagger()
                    _trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
                    _trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_stagger)
                    _spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_STAGGER_FX, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
                else:
                    _trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
                    _trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_parry)
                    _spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
            return
    Then the player-hit fall-through (kind strong+block, or no defense): keep existing
    regen + flash + particles + shake, and ADD: player.reset_combo() and
    _trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak).
  - _resolve_player_swing(): threat_* unchanged (return). idle branch unchanged (strong
    drains fatigue; return). Replace the recovery/exhausted hit branch to also include
    staggered and apply riposte/execute multipliers + combo + hitstop:
        if es == &"recovery_weak" or es == &"recovery_strong" or es == &"exhausted" or es == &"staggered":
            var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
            var dmg: float = float(base_dmg)
            var is_riposte: bool = player.is_riposte_ready()
            if is_riposte:
                dmg *= Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier
            var is_execute: bool = enemy.hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp
            if is_execute:
                dmg *= Balance.execute_damage_multiplier
            enemy.damage_hp(int(round(dmg)))
            if is_riposte:
                player.consume_riposte()
            player.add_combo()
            # juice
            var big: bool = is_riposte or is_execute or kind == &"strong"
            _trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG if big else SHAKE_HIT_WEAK)
            _trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_strong if big else Balance.hitstop_weak)
            enemy.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
            var fx_col: Color = COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX if is_riposte else COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE
            _spawn_particles(enemy.position, fx_col, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
            return
  - _update_hud(): call hud.update with the 3 new trailing args per contract.
  - game_over restart path (top of _on_tick) still calls _update_hud(); fine.

--- hud.gd ---
  - Change update() to the new 11-arg signature; store combo + rank + max_combo in new
    vars (var combo: int = 0; var combo_rank: StringName = &""; var max_combo: int = 0).
  - In _on_canvas_draw(): draw the combo at top-center when combo >= 1:
      a big number like "x{combo}" plus the rank letter, colored by rank.
      Rank colors (add consts): C = Color(0.95,0.9,0.4), B = Color(0.4,0.85,1.0),
      A = Color(1.0,0.7,0.2), S = Color(1.0,0.35,0.35). None -> dim.
      Place around y = 90, centered horizontally (VIEWPORT_W). Font sizes: combo ~46,
      rank letter ~34. Use ThemeDB.fallback_font like existing code.
  - On the win overlay, under the existing hint, add a line:
      "Max combo: {max_combo}  [{rank for max_combo}]" centered, font ~28.
      Compute the rank-for-max-combo inline with the same thresholds (or just show the
      stored combo_rank if you prefer — but max_combo rank is nicer; compute inline).
  - Keep all existing debug text + win text behavior.

--- gameplay.md + plan.md + sprites/SPRITES.md (docs owner) ---
  Sync the docs to the above. Specifically:
  gameplay.md:
    - Player section: document riposte (active parry arms riposte; next damaging hit x1.6;
      window riposte_window_ticks) and perfect parry (block started within perfect_parry_ticks
      -> enemy staggered) + the new staggered enemy state in the Enemy state table
      (staggered: stagger_ticks, wide open, all hits land, cannot act).
    - Enemy transitions: add feint branch (weak threat, feint_probability -> no swing ->
      short idle feint_idle_ticks -> re-decide) and the perfect-parry -> staggered branch.
    - Swing resolve (player): note riposte multiplier and execute multiplier
      (enemy.hp <= execute_threshold_hp -> x1.5) and that staggered counts as a hit window.
    - Add Combo & Rank section (rules + tiers). Add Execute rule.
    - Update the FSM summary diagrams (enemy: add staggered + feint; player: note riposte arm).
    - Note hit-stop + tints are polish (presentation only).
  plan.md:
    - Update the balance.json JSON block to include the new player/enemy_basic/fight keys
      and the new fx section with the shipped defaults.
    - Add the new keys to the tunable reference tables (player / enemy_basic / fight + a new
      fx group for hit-stop).
    - Polish layer: add hit-stop (freeze frames), readability tints (threat pulse, stagger
      cyan, execute red, player riposte gold).
    - HUD: note combo + rank top-center, max combo on win.
    - Enemy SPRITE_INDEX: note staggered reuses exhausted cell (idx 5) + cyan tint.
    - Add verification scenarios for: perfect parry -> stagger, riposte bonus damage, feint
      bait, combo build + reset, execute bonus under 25 HP, hit-stop freeze on strong/parry.
  sprites/SPRITES.md:
    - Add a short note: staggered (enemy) reuses cell idx 5 with cyan modulate tint; threat
      onset pulse tint; execute red tint when hp <= execute_threshold_hp; player riposte-ready
      gold tint. All modulate-only, no new cells. Document the modulate priority order.
  Keep values consistent with balance.json defaults above. Do NOT edit any .gd files.

================ CORRECTNESS NOTES ================
  - Tick order in fight._on_tick: player.tick(); enemy.tick(); player.handle_input();
    resolve enemy swing; resolve player swing; fatigue->exhausted; win check; hud.
    is_perfect_parry()/is_active_block() are evaluated at enemy-swing-resolve time, AFTER
    player.tick() (which incremented blocking_ticks_elapsed) and AFTER handle_input (which
    may have just entered blocking this tick with blocking_ticks_elapsed = 0 -> perfect).
  - riposte multiplier x execute multiplier stack; round once at the end with int(round()).
  - Damage uses Balance ints; do float math then int(round(...)).
  - Do not introduce physics/Area2D. Do not add AnimationPlayer. GDScript 4.x syntax.
`

const FILE_TASK_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['ownedFiles', 'changesSummary', 'contractConcerns', 'newSymbols', 'done'],
  properties: {
    ownedFiles: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    changesSummary: { type: 'string', description: 'concise bullet summary of edits made' },
    newSymbols: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'new vars/consts/funcs added (names)' },
    contractConcerns: { type: 'string', description: 'anything where the spec was ambiguous or a contract mismatch risk — "" if none' },
    done: { type: 'boolean', description: 'true if all assigned edits were applied' },
  },
}

const ASSIGNMENTS = [
  {
    key: 'balance',
    files: [REPO + '\\balance.json', REPO + '\\balance.gd'],
    task: 'Add all new balance keys to balance.json (new player/enemy_basic/fight keys + new "fx" section) with the shipped defaults, and declare + parse matching vars in balance.gd. Use int() for ints and float() for riposte_damage_multiplier, feint_probability, execute_damage_multiplier, and the four hitstop floats. Match the existing JSON formatting style and the existing balance.gd loader structure.',
  },
  {
    key: 'player',
    files: [REPO + '\\player.gd'],
    task: 'Implement the player.gd section: riposte/combo vars + methods (exact contract signatures), tick() decrements for riposte_ready_ticks and combo timer, the SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE const, and the _update_sprite() modulate priority. Do not change input/attack/dodge logic.',
  },
  {
    key: 'enemy',
    files: [REPO + '\\enemy.gd', REPO + '\\enemy_basic.gd'],
    task: 'Implement the enemy.gd + enemy_basic.gd section: staggered state (SPRITE_INDEX idx 5, enter_stagger, _advance_state_after_expiry case), feint logic (is_feint, _decide_attack roll, threat_weak expiry branch, guard the defensive swing branch with "and not is_feint", clear in _enter_idle), threat_pulse_ticks + the new tint consts, _update_sprite() modulate priority (hit_flash > staggered > threat pulse > execute > white), and the base + enemy_basic tunable accessors.',
  },
  {
    key: 'fight',
    files: [REPO + '\\fight.gd'],
    task: 'Implement the fight.gd section: hitstop_timer + the new _process pump that freezes ticks during hitstop, _trigger_hitstop helper, COLOR_STAGGER_FX/COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX consts, the rewritten _resolve_enemy_swing (active parry arms riposte; perfect parry staggers; player-hit resets combo + small hitstop), the rewritten _resolve_player_swing hit branch (staggered included; riposte+execute multipliers; combo; hitstop; fx), and the _update_hud call with the 3 new trailing args. Match the exact contract names.',
  },
  {
    key: 'hud',
    files: [REPO + '\\hud.gd'],
    task: 'Implement the hud.gd section: the new 11-arg update() signature + new vars (combo, combo_rank, max_combo), the top-center combo+rank draw (rank-colored, only when combo >= 1), and the max-combo line on the win overlay. Keep all existing debug/win drawing.',
  },
  {
    key: 'docs',
    files: [REPO + '\\gameplay.md', REPO + '\\plan.md', REPO + '\\sprites\\SPRITES.md'],
    task: 'Sync the docs per the spec docs section: gameplay.md (riposte/perfect-parry/stagger, feints, execute, combo+rank, updated FSM diagrams, swing resolve), plan.md (balance.json block + tunable tables + fx group, polish layer hit-stop + tints, HUD combo, staggered cell reuse, new verification scenarios), and sprites/SPRITES.md (modulate-only new visuals + priority order). Keep values consistent with the shipped defaults. Do NOT edit any .gd or .json files.',
  },
]

phase('Implement')
const results = await parallel(ASSIGNMENTS.map(a => () =>
  agent(
    'You are implementing one slice of a frozen, fully-specified feature package for an existing Godot 4.6 game. Read your assigned file(s) FIRST, then apply EXACTLY the edits the spec assigns to your file(s) — no more, no less. Preserve existing code style, indentation (tabs), and comment language (Czech+English mix is fine). Use the Edit/Write tools to actually modify the files. Cross-file names MUST match the PUBLIC CONTRACT verbatim. After editing, return your structured summary.\n\n' +
    'YOUR OWNED FILE(S): ' + a.files.join(', ') + '\n' +
    'YOUR TASK: ' + a.task + '\n\n' +
    '================ FULL SPEC ================\n' + SPEC,
    { label: 'impl:' + a.key, phase: 'Implement', schema: FILE_TASK_SCHEMA }
  ).then(r => r ? { key: a.key, ...r } : { key: a.key, failed: true })
))

return results

content.text: Všech 6 agentů hlásí hotovo. Teď ověřím skutečný kód — přečtu si modifikované soubory a paralelně spustím adversariální review.

content.content:
1	extends Node2D
2	
3	# Fight scene controller. Owns the 16 Hz tick accumulator, calls combatant
4	# ticks in order, resolves swing events, updates the HUD, checks win condition.
5	
6	const TICKS_PER_SECOND := 16
7	const TICK_DURATION := 1.0 / TICKS_PER_SECOND
8	
9	# Gameplay resolve numbers (damage, fatigue, regen, hit flash) live in
10	# `balance.json` and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual
11	# polish constants (shake/particles) remain as `const` below.
12	
13	# Polish fx — purely visual, no gameplay impact.
14	const SHAKE_HIT_WEAK := 4.0
15	const SHAKE_HIT_STRONG := 10.0
16	const SHAKE_FATIGUE := 3.0
17	const SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC := 30.0
18	const PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE := 10
19	const PARTICLES_FATIGUE := 6
20	const PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS := 0.45
21	const PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN := 80.0
22	const PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX := 220.0
23	const PARTICLE_SIZE := 4.0
24	const PARTICLE_DRAG := 0.92  # velocity multiplier per frame at 60 fps reference
25	const COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE := Color(1.0, 0.5, 0.3, 1.0)
26	const COLOR_FATIGUE_FX := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
27	const COLOR_PLAYER_HIT := Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.3, 1.0)
28	const COLOR_STAGGER_FX := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
29	const COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX := Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
30	
31	@onready var player: Node2D = $Player
32	@onready var enemy: Node2D = $Enemy
33	@onready var hud: CanvasLayer = $HUD
34	@onready var background_canvas: CanvasLayer = $Background
35	
36	var tick_acc: float = 0.0
37	var game_over: bool = false
38	
39	# Hit-stop freeze timer (real seconds). While > 0 the tick loop is frozen but
40	# shake/particles/redraw keep updating — pure presentation, gameplay-neutral.
41	var hitstop_timer: float = 0.0
42	
43	# Polish fx state.
44	var shake_amount: float = 0.0
45	var rng_fx := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
46	# Each particle: {pos: Vector2, vel: Vector2, life: float, color: Color}
47	var particles: Array = []
48	
49	
50	func _process(delta: float) -> void:
51		if hitstop_timer > 0.0:
52			hitstop_timer = maxf(0.0, hitstop_timer - delta)
53		else:
54			tick_acc += delta
55			while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION and hitstop_timer <= 0.0:
56				tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
57				_on_tick()
58		_update_shake(delta)
59		_update_particles(delta)
60		queue_redraw()
61	
62	
63	func _update_shake(delta: float) -> void:
64		if shake_amount <= 0.0:
65			position = Vector2.ZERO
66			background_canvas.offset = Vector2.ZERO
67			return
68		var dir := Vector2(
69			rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0),
70			rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
71		)
72		if dir.length() > 0.001:
73			dir = dir.normalized()
74		position = dir * shake_amount
75		# Mirror shake into the background CanvasLayer (it's outside the Fight
76		# Node2D transform, so offset is the only way to shake it).
77		background_canvas.offset = position
78		shake_amount = maxf(0.0, shake_amount - SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC * delta)
79	
80	
81	func _update_particles(delta: float) -> void:
82		for i in range(particles.size() - 1, -1, -1):
83			var p: Dictionary = particles[i]
84			p.life -= delta
85			if p.life <= 0.0:
86				particles.remove_at(i)
87				continue
88			p.pos += p.vel * delta
89			p.vel *= PARTICLE_DRAG
90	
91	
92	func _trigger_shake(amount: float) -> void:
93		# Combine — don't downgrade an already-bigger shake in progress.
94		shake_amount = maxf(shake_amount, amount)
95	
96	
97	func _trigger_hitstop(amount: float) -> void:
98		# Combine — don't shorten an already-longer freeze in progress.
99		hitstop_timer = maxf(hitstop_timer, amount)
100	
101	
102	func _spawn_particles(at: Vector2, color: Color, count: int) -> void:
103		for _i in count:
104			var angle := rng_fx.randf_range(-PI, PI)
105			var speed := rng_fx.randf_range(PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN, PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX)
106			var vel := Vector2(cos(angle), sin(angle)) * speed
107			particles.append({
108				"pos": at,
109				"vel": vel,
110				"life": PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS,
111				"color": color,
112			})
113	
114	
115	func _between_combatants() -> Vector2:
116		return (player.position + enemy.position) * 0.5
117	
118	
119	func _draw() -> void:
120		for p: Dictionary in particles:
121			var alpha := clampf(p.life / PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS, 0.0, 1.0)
122			var col: Color = p.color
123			col.a = alpha
124			var s := PARTICLE_SIZE
125			draw_rect(Rect2(p.pos - Vector2(s, s) * 0.5, Vector2(s, s)), col)
126	
127	
128	func _on_tick() -> void:
129		if game_over:
130			# In win state — only listen for restart.
131			if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):
132				get_tree().reload_current_scene()
133			_update_hud()
134			return
135	
136		# Tick order: player first, enemy second. (Doesn't materially matter because
137		# resolution happens after both have ticked, but keeps deterministic.)
138		player.tick()
139		enemy.tick()
140	
141		# Player input handled at tick boundary (post-countdown so freshly-finished
142		# states allow new input on the same tick).
143		player.handle_input()
144	
145		# Resolve enemy swing first (enemy attack lands on player).
146		if enemy.swing_this_tick:
147			_resolve_enemy_swing()
148	
149		# Then player swing (player attack lands on enemy).
150		if player.swing_this_tick:
151			_resolve_player_swing()
152	
153		# Fatigue → exhausted transition (enemy only — player has no fatigue).
154		if enemy.fatigue <= 0 and enemy.state != &"exhausted":
155			enemy.enter_exhausted()
156	
157		# Win check.
158		if enemy.hp <= 0:
159			game_over = true
160	
161		_update_hud()
162	
163	
164	# --- Resolves ------------------------------------------------------------
165	
166	func _resolve_enemy_swing() -> void:
167		# Enemy swing event on the last tick of threat_*.
168		var kind: StringName = enemy.pending_attack_kind
169	
170		# 1. Player dodging (invincible window) → miss.
171		if player.state == &"dodging" and player.dodge_invincible:
172			return
173	
174		# 2. Weak attack + player blocking → block. Active parry drains enemy fatigue
175		# AND arms a riposte on the player. Perfect parry (block started within
176		# perfect_parry_ticks) additionally staggers the enemy. Passive block (held
177		# too long) just protects: no drain, no riposte.
178		if kind == &"weak" and player.state == &"blocking":
179			if player.is_active_block():
180				enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
181				player.set_riposte_ready()
182				if player.is_perfect_parry():
183					enemy.enter_stagger()
184					_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
185					_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_stagger)
186					_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_STAGGER_FX, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
187				else:
188					_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
189					_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_parry)
190					_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
191			return
192	
193		# 3. Strong attack + player blocking → block ineffective (fall through).
194		# 4. Otherwise → player hit (player invincible, but enemy regens).
195		enemy.heal_hp(Balance.enemy_hp_regen_on_hit)
196		enemy.add_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit)
197		_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_WEAK if kind == &"weak" else SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
198		player.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
199		_spawn_particles(player.position, COLOR_PLAYER_HIT, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
200		# Player got hit: break combo and a small hit-stop punch.
201		player.reset_combo()
202		_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)
203	
204	
205	func _resolve_player_swing() -> void:
206		var kind: StringName = player.pending_attack_kind
207		var es: StringName = enemy.state
208	
209		# Enemy in threat_* → no effect (telegraph cannot be interrupted).
210		if es == &"threat_weak" or es == &"threat_strong":
211			return
212	
213		# Enemy in idle → blocks player attacks, with strong draining fatigue.
214		if es == &"idle":
215			if kind == &"strong":
216				enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle)
217				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
218				_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
219			# TODO: future visual block feedback could be a temporary sprite swap
220			# (e.g. a `block_react` stance cell) on enemy.gd. For now no extra fx.
221			return
222	
223		# Enemy in recovery_*, exhausted, or staggered → hit lands. Riposte and
224		# execute multipliers stack multiplicatively; round once at the end.
225		if es == &"recovery_weak" or es == &"recovery_strong" or es == &"exhausted" or es == &"staggered":
226			var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
227			var dmg: float = float(base_dmg)
228			var is_riposte: bool = player.is_riposte_ready()
229			if is_riposte:
230				dmg *= Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier
231			var is_execute: bool = enemy.hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp
232			if is_execute:
233				dmg *= Balance.execute_damage_multiplier
234			enemy.damage_hp(int(round(dmg)))
235			if is_riposte:
236				player.consume_riposte()
237			player.add_combo()
238			# juice
239			var big: bool = is_riposte or is_execute or kind == &"strong"
240			_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG if big else SHAKE_HIT_WEAK)
241			_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_strong if big else Balance.hitstop_weak)
242			enemy.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
243			var fx_col: Color = COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX if is_riposte else COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE
244			_spawn_particles(enemy.position, fx_col, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
245			return
246	
247		# Fallback (shouldn't normally hit): treat as blocked.
248	
249	
250	func _update_hud() -> void:
251		# State-ticks field is repurposed per-state for visibility:
252		#   blocking → active parry window remaining (0..Balance.block_active_ticks)
253		#   other    → state_ticks_remaining
254		var p_ticks: int = player.state_ticks_remaining
255		if player.state == &"blocking":
256			p_ticks = player.active_block_ticks_remaining()
257		hud.update(
258			enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
259			player.state, p_ticks,
260			player.dodge_cooldown_ticks, game_over,
261			player.combo, player.combo_rank(), player.max_combo
262		)
263

extends Node2D

# Fight scene controller. Owns the 16 Hz tick accumulator, calls combatant
# ticks in order, resolves swing events, updates the HUD, checks win condition.

const TICKS_PER_SECOND := 16
const TICK_DURATION := 1.0 / TICKS_PER_SECOND

# Gameplay resolve numbers (damage, fatigue, regen, hit flash) live in
# `balance.json` and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual
# polish constants (shake/particles) remain as `const` below.

# Polish fx — purely visual, no gameplay impact.
const SHAKE_HIT_WEAK := 4.0
const SHAKE_HIT_STRONG := 10.0
const SHAKE_FATIGUE := 3.0
const SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC := 30.0
const PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE := 10
const PARTICLES_FATIGUE := 6
const PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS := 0.45
const PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN := 80.0
const PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX := 220.0
const PARTICLE_SIZE := 4.0
const PARTICLE_DRAG := 0.92  # velocity multiplier per frame at 60 fps reference
const COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE := Color(1.0, 0.5, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_FATIGUE_FX := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_PLAYER_HIT := Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_STAGGER_FX := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
const COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX := Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)

@onready var player: Node2D = $Player
@onready var enemy: Node2D = $Enemy
@onready var hud: CanvasLayer = $HUD
@onready var background_canvas: CanvasLayer = $Background

var tick_acc: float = 0.0
var game_over: bool = false

# Hit-stop freeze timer (real seconds). While > 0 the tick loop is frozen but
# shake/particles/redraw keep updating — pure presentation, gameplay-neutral.
var hitstop_timer: float = 0.0

# Polish fx state.
var shake_amount: float = 0.0
var rng_fx := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
# Each particle: {pos: Vector2, vel: Vector2, life: float, color: Color}
var particles: Array = []


func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	if hitstop_timer > 0.0:
		hitstop_timer = maxf(0.0, hitstop_timer - delta)
	else:
		tick_acc += delta
		while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION and hitstop_timer <= 0.0:
			tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
			_on_tick()
	_update_shake(delta)
	_update_particles(delta)
	queue_redraw()


func _update_shake(delta: float) -> void:
	if shake_amount <= 0.0:
		position = Vector2.ZERO
		background_canvas.offset = Vector2.ZERO
		return
	var dir := Vector2(
		rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0),
		rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
	)
	if dir.length() > 0.001:
		dir = dir.normalized()
	position = dir * shake_amount
	# Mirror shake into the background CanvasLayer (it's outside the Fight
	# Node2D transform, so offset is the only way to shake it).
	background_canvas.offset = position
	shake_amount = maxf(0.0, shake_amount - SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC * delta)


func _update_particles(delta: float) -> void:
	for i in range(particles.size() - 1, -1, -1):
		var p: Dictionary = particles[i]
		p.life -= delta
		if p.life <= 0.0:
			particles.remove_at(i)
			continue
		p.pos += p.vel * delta
		p.vel *= PARTICLE_DRAG


func _trigger_shake(amount: float) -> void:
	# Combine — don't downgrade an already-bigger shake in progress.
	shake_amount = maxf(shake_amount, amount)


func _trigger_hitstop(amount: float) -> void:
	# Combine — don't shorten an already-longer freeze in progress.
	hitstop_timer = maxf(hitstop_timer, amount)


func _spawn_particles(at: Vector2, color: Color, count: int) -> void:
	for _i in count:
		var angle := rng_fx.randf_range(-PI, PI)
		var speed := rng_fx.randf_range(PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN, PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX)
		var vel := Vector2(cos(angle), sin(angle)) * speed
		particles.append({
			"pos": at,
			"vel": vel,
			"life": PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS,
			"color": color,
		})


func _between_combatants() -> Vector2:
	return (player.position + enemy.position) * 0.5


func _draw() -> void:
	for p: Dictionary in particles:
		var alpha := clampf(p.life / PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS, 0.0, 1.0)
		var col: Color = p.color
		col.a = alpha
		var s := PARTICLE_SIZE
		draw_rect(Rect2(p.pos - Vector2(s, s) * 0.5, Vector2(s, s)), col)


func _on_tick() -> void:
	if game_over:
		# In win state — only listen for restart.
		if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):
			get_tree().reload_current_scene()
		_update_hud()
		return

	# Tick order: player first, enemy second. (Doesn't materially matter because
	# resolution happens after both have ticked, but keeps deterministic.)
	player.tick()
	enemy.tick()

	# Player input handled at tick boundary (post-countdown so freshly-finished
	# states allow new input on the same tick).
	player.handle_input()

	# Resolve enemy swing first (enemy attack lands on player).
	if enemy.swing_this_tick:
		_resolve_enemy_swing()

	# Then player swing (player attack lands on enemy).
	if player.swing_this_tick:
		_resolve_player_swing()

	# Fatigue → exhausted transition (enemy only — player has no fatigue).
	if enemy.fatigue <= 0 and enemy.state != &"exhausted":
		enemy.enter_exhausted()

	# Win check.
	if enemy.hp <= 0:
		game_over = true

	_update_hud()


# --- Resolves ------------------------------------------------------------

func _resolve_enemy_swing() -> void:
	# Enemy swing event on the last tick of threat_*.
	var kind: StringName = enemy.pending_attack_kind

	# 1. Player dodging (invincible window) → miss.
	if player.state == &"dodging" and player.dodge_invincible:
		return

	# 2. Weak attack + player blocking → block. Active parry drains enemy fatigue
	# AND arms a riposte on the player. Perfect parry (block started within
	# perfect_parry_ticks) additionally staggers the enemy. Passive block (held
	# too long) just protects: no drain, no riposte.
	if kind == &"weak" and player.state == &"blocking":
		if player.is_active_block():
			enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
			player.set_riposte_ready()
			if player.is_perfect_parry():
				enemy.enter_stagger()
				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
				_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_stagger)
				_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_STAGGER_FX, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
			else:
				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
				_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_parry)
				_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
		return

	# 3. Strong attack + player blocking → block ineffective (fall through).
	# 4. Otherwise → player hit (player invincible, but enemy regens).
	enemy.heal_hp(Balance.enemy_hp_regen_on_hit)
	enemy.add_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit)
	_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_WEAK if kind == &"weak" else SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
	player.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
	_spawn_particles(player.position, COLOR_PLAYER_HIT, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
	# Player got hit: break combo and a small hit-stop punch.
	player.reset_combo()
	_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)


func _resolve_player_swing() -> void:
	var kind: StringName = player.pending_attack_kind
	var es: StringName = enemy.state

	# Enemy in threat_* → no effect (telegraph cannot be interrupted).
	if es == &"threat_weak" or es == &"threat_strong":
		return

	# Enemy in idle → blocks player attacks, with strong draining fatigue.
	if es == &"idle":
		if kind == &"strong":
			enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle)
			_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
			_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
		# TODO: future visual block feedback could be a temporary sprite swap
		# (e.g. a `block_react` stance cell) on enemy.gd. For now no extra fx.
		return

	# Enemy in recovery_*, exhausted, or staggered → hit lands. Riposte and
	# execute multipliers stack multiplicatively; round once at the end.
	if es == &"recovery_weak" or es == &"recovery_strong" or es == &"exhausted" or es == &"staggered":
		var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
		var dmg: float = float(base_dmg)
		var is_riposte: bool = player.is_riposte_ready()
		if is_riposte:
			dmg *= Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier
		var is_execute: bool = enemy.hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp
		if is_execute:
			dmg *= Balance.execute_damage_multiplier
		enemy.damage_hp(int(round(dmg)))
		if is_riposte:
			player.consume_riposte()
		player.add_combo()
		# juice
		var big: bool = is_riposte or is_execute or kind == &"strong"
		_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG if big else SHAKE_HIT_WEAK)
		_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_strong if big else Balance.hitstop_weak)
		enemy.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
		var fx_col: Color = COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX if is_riposte else COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE
		_spawn_particles(enemy.position, fx_col, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
		return

	# Fallback (shouldn't normally hit): treat as blocked.


func _update_hud() -> void:
	# State-ticks field is repurposed per-state for visibility:
	#   blocking → active parry window remaining (0..Balance.block_active_ticks)
	#   other    → state_ticks_remaining
	var p_ticks: int = player.state_ticks_remaining
	if player.state == &"blocking":
		p_ticks = player.active_block_ticks_remaining()
	hud.update(
		enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
		player.state, p_ticks,
		player.dodge_cooldown_ticks, game_over,
		player.combo, player.combo_rank(), player.max_combo
	)

content.content:
1	extends Combatant
2	
3	# Player FSM. Input drives state transitions. Swing event lands on the last
4	# tick of the windup; fight.gd reads `swing_this_tick` + `pending_attack_kind`.
5	
6	# Gameplay tunables (timing, cooldowns, parry window) live in `balance.json`
7	# and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual / layout constants
8	# remain as `const` below.
9	
10	# --- Sprite rendering ----------------------------------------------------
11	# Player body = Sprite2D with region_rect cell-picker. Each FSM stance
12	# (including derived sub-stances) maps to exactly one cell in the strip.
13	# A second Sprite2D (weapon) overlays the body using the same cell index —
14	# weapon sheet has the same layout, just a separate texture.
15	# See sprites/SPRITES.md for the per-cell stance spec.
16	const SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/player.png")
17	const WEAPON_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/player_weapon.png")
18	const SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(32, 32)
19	const SPRITE_SCALE := 8.0
20	const SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE := Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)
21	# Golden brightened tint while a riposte is armed.
22	const SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE := Color(1.6, 1.35, 0.4, 1.0)
23	const SPRITE_INDEX := {
24		&"idle": 0,
25		&"attacking_weak": 1,
26		&"attacking_strong_windup": 2,
27		&"attacking_strong_recovery": 3,
28		&"blocking_active": 4,
29		&"blocking_passive": 5,
30		&"dodging": 6,
31	}
32	
33	var sprite: Sprite2D
34	var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D
35	
36	# Set true for exactly one tick when player executes a swing event.
37	var swing_this_tick: bool = false
38	# &"weak" or &"strong" — read on swing tick.
39	var pending_attack_kind: StringName = &"weak"
40	
41	# Remaining ticks of the windup portion of an attack. When this hits 0 and
42	# state is attacking_*, swing fires on that tick and recovery begins.
43	var windup_ticks_remaining: int = 0
44	
45	# Ticks elapsed since entering &"blocking". Used to compute active parry window.
46	var blocking_ticks_elapsed: int = 0
47	
48	# Tracks dodge cooldown (separate from state countdown so we can be in idle
49	# while still on cooldown).
50	var dodge_cooldown_ticks: int = 0
51	
52	# Tracks weak-attack cooldown (counted alongside the attacking_weak state — by
53	# the time we're back in idle, this still has a few ticks left to gate spam).
54	var weak_cooldown_ticks: int = 0
55	
56	# True while we're in the invincibility portion of dodging.
57	var dodge_invincible: bool = false
58	
59	# --- Riposte & combo -----------------------------------------------------
60	# Active parry arms a riposte: while riposte_ready_ticks > 0, the next damaging
61	# hit is multiplied by Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier, then consumed.
62	var riposte_ready_ticks: int = 0
63	# Combo = consecutive damaging hits; resets on getting hit or on timeout.
64	var combo: int = 0
65	var combo_timer_ticks: int = 0
66	var max_combo: int = 0
67	
68	
69	func _ready() -> void:
70		sprite = _make_sprite(SPRITE_SHEET)
71		add_child(sprite)
72		# weapon_sprite added after body → drawn on top in same _draw pass.
73		weapon_sprite = _make_sprite(WEAPON_SHEET)
74		add_child(weapon_sprite)
75		set_state(&"idle", 0)
76		_update_sprite()
77	
78	
79	func _make_sprite(sheet: Texture2D) -> Sprite2D:
80		var s := Sprite2D.new()
81		s.texture = sheet
82		s.region_enabled = true
83		s.region_rect = Rect2(0, 0, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
84		s.scale = Vector2(SPRITE_SCALE, SPRITE_SCALE)
85		s.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
86		return s
87	
88	
89	# Called by fight.gd in tick order BEFORE input handling each tick so countdown
90	# advances first. Input handling lives separately in handle_input().
91	func tick() -> void:
92		swing_this_tick = false
93		if dodge_cooldown_ticks > 0:
94			dodge_cooldown_ticks -= 1
95		if weak_cooldown_ticks > 0:
96			weak_cooldown_ticks -= 1
97	
98		# Riposte window expires on its own; sprite is refreshed at end of tick.
99		if riposte_ready_ticks > 0:
100			riposte_ready_ticks -= 1
101	
102		# Combo decays if no damaging hit lands within combo_timeout_ticks.
103		if combo_timer_ticks > 0:
104			combo_timer_ticks -= 1
105			if combo_timer_ticks == 0:
106				combo = 0
107	
108		# Attack states use a separate windup counter so we can fire the swing
109		# event on the last windup tick, then continue into recovery within the
110		# same `attacking_*` state.
111		if state == &"attacking_weak" or state == &"attacking_strong":
112			if windup_ticks_remaining > 0:
113				windup_ticks_remaining -= 1
114				if windup_ticks_remaining == 0:
115					# This tick IS the last windup tick — swing now.
116					swing_this_tick = true
117	
118		# Track block age for parry window — only the first block_active_ticks of a
119		# block drain enemy fatigue.
120		if state == &"blocking":
121			blocking_ticks_elapsed += 1
122	
123		# Update dodge phase tracking before generic countdown.
124		if state == &"dodging":
125			# Invincibility lasts the first dodge_invincibility ticks of the
126			# 8-tick state. state_ticks_remaining starts at 8 and decrements.
127			var elapsed: int = Balance.dodge_invincibility + Balance.dodge_recover - state_ticks_remaining
128			dodge_invincible = elapsed < Balance.dodge_invincibility
129	
130		super.tick()
131	
132		if state_expired_this_tick:
133			_on_state_expired()
134	
135		_update_sprite()
136	
137	
138	func _on_state_expired() -> void:
139		match state:
140			&"attacking_weak", &"attacking_strong", &"dodging":
141				dodge_invincible = false
142				set_state(&"idle", 0)
143			_:
144				pass
145	
146	
147	# Called from fight.gd._on_tick() AFTER tick() so input is sampled at tick
148	# boundaries (not per-frame). This keeps everything deterministic on the tick
149	# grid.
150	func handle_input() -> void:
151		# Inputs are only accepted in idle or blocking states. attacking_* and
152		# dodging swallow input until they finish.
153		# v1 keyboard-only mapping: arrow keys. Right = fast weak attack, Up =
154		# strong attack, Down (hold) = block, Left (tap) = dodge.
155		match state:
156			&"idle":
157				# Block has highest priority (held) — must check before tap actions
158				# so player can transition out of attacks-finished into block on
159				# the same tick.
160				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_DOWN):
161					set_state(&"blocking", 0)
162					blocking_ticks_elapsed = 0
163					_update_sprite()
164					return
165				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_RIGHT) and weak_cooldown_ticks == 0:
166					_begin_attack(&"weak")
167					return
168				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_UP):
169					_begin_attack(&"strong")
170					return
171				if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_LEFT) and dodge_cooldown_ticks == 0:
172					_begin_dodge()
173					return
174			&"blocking":
175				if not Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_DOWN):
176					set_state(&"idle", 0)
177					_update_sprite()
178	
179	
180	func _begin_attack(kind: StringName) -> void:
181		pending_attack_kind = kind
182		var windup: int
183		var recover: int
184		if kind == &"weak":
185			windup = Balance.attack_weak_windup
186			recover = Balance.attack_weak_recover
187			set_state(&"attacking_weak", windup + recover)
188			weak_cooldown_ticks = Balance.attack_weak_cooldown
189		else:
190			windup = Balance.attack_strong_windup
191			recover = Balance.attack_strong_recover
192			set_state(&"attacking_strong", windup + recover)
193		windup_ticks_remaining = windup
194	
195		# Zero-windup attacks (weak) swing instantly this tick.
196		if windup == 0:
197			swing_this_tick = true
198		_update_sprite()
199	
200	
201	func _begin_dodge() -> void:
202		set_state(&"dodging", Balance.dodge_invincibility + Balance.dodge_recover)
203		dodge_invincible = true
204		dodge_cooldown_ticks = Balance.dodge_cooldown
205		_update_sprite()
206	
207	
208	func is_active_block() -> bool:
209		return state == &"blocking" and blocking_ticks_elapsed < Balance.block_active_ticks
210	
211	
212	func active_block_ticks_remaining() -> int:
213		if state != &"blocking":
214			return 0
215		return maxi(0, Balance.block_active_ticks - blocking_ticks_elapsed)
216	
217	
218	# --- Riposte -------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	func set_riposte_ready() -> void:
221		riposte_ready_ticks = Balance.riposte_window_ticks
222		_update_sprite()
223	
224	
225	func is_riposte_ready() -> bool:
226		return riposte_ready_ticks > 0
227	
228	
229	func consume_riposte() -> void:
230		riposte_ready_ticks = 0
231		_update_sprite()
232	
233	
234	# Perfect parry = block was started within the last perfect_parry_ticks ticks
235	# at the moment the weak swing resolves (strict subset of active parry).
236	func is_perfect_parry() -> bool:
237		return state == &"blocking" and blocking_ticks_elapsed <= Balance.perfect_parry_ticks
238	
239	
240	# --- Combo & rank --------------------------------------------------------
241	
242	func add_combo() -> void:
243		combo += 1
244		max_combo = maxi(max_combo, combo)
245		combo_timer_ticks = Balance.combo_timeout_ticks
246	
247	
248	func reset_combo() -> void:
249		combo = 0
250		combo_timer_ticks = 0
251	
252	
253	func combo_rank() -> StringName:
254		if combo >= 15:
255			return &"S"
256		elif combo >= 10:
257			return &"A"
258		elif combo >= 5:
259			return &"B"
260		elif combo >= 1:
261			return &"C"
262		return &""
263	
264	
265	# --- Sprite helpers ------------------------------------------------------
266	
267	func _current_visual_stance() -> StringName:
268		match state:
269			&"attacking_strong":
270				return &"attacking_strong_windup" if windup_ticks_remaining > 0 else &"attacking_strong_recovery"
271			&"blocking":
272				return &"blocking_active" if is_active_block() else &"blocking_passive"
273			_:
274				return state
275	
276	
277	func _update_sprite() -> void:
278		var idx: int = SPRITE_INDEX.get(_current_visual_stance(), 0)
279		var rect := Rect2(idx * SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, 0,
280				SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
281		# Modulate priority: hit_flash > riposte-ready > WHITE.
282		var mod: Color
283		if hit_flash_ticks > 0:
284			mod = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE
285		elif riposte_ready_ticks > 0:
286			mod = SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE
287		else:
288			mod = Color.WHITE
289		sprite.region_rect = rect
290		sprite.modulate = mod
291		weapon_sprite.region_rect = rect
292		weapon_sprite.modulate = mod

file.content:
extends Combatant

# Player FSM. Input drives state transitions. Swing event lands on the last
# tick of the windup; fight.gd reads `swing_this_tick` + `pending_attack_kind`.

# Gameplay tunables (timing, cooldowns, parry window) live in `balance.json`
# and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual / layout constants
# remain as `const` below.

# --- Sprite rendering ----------------------------------------------------
# Player body = Sprite2D with region_rect cell-picker. Each FSM stance
# (including derived sub-stances) maps to exactly one cell in the strip.
# A second Sprite2D (weapon) overlays the body using the same cell index —
# weapon sheet has the same layout, just a separate texture.
# See sprites/SPRITES.md for the per-cell stance spec.
const SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/player.png")
const WEAPON_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/player_weapon.png")
const SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(32, 32)
const SPRITE_SCALE := 8.0
const SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE := Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)
# Golden brightened tint while a riposte is armed.
const SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE := Color(1.6, 1.35, 0.4, 1.0)
const SPRITE_INDEX := {
	&"idle": 0,
	&"attacking_weak": 1,
	&"attacking_strong_windup": 2,
	&"attacking_strong_recovery": 3,
	&"blocking_active": 4,
	&"blocking_passive": 5,
	&"dodging": 6,
}

var sprite: Sprite2D
var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D

# Set true for exactly one tick when player executes a swing event.
var swing_this_tick: bool = false
# &"weak" or &"strong" — read on swing tick.
var pending_attack_kind: StringName = &"weak"

# Remaining ticks of the windup portion of an attack. When this hits 0 and
# state is attacking_*, swing fires on that tick and recovery begins.
var windup_ticks_remaining: int = 0

# Ticks elapsed since entering &"blocking". Used to compute active parry window.
var blocking_ticks_elapsed: int = 0

# Tracks dodge cooldown (separate from state countdown so we can be in idle
# while still on cooldown).
var dodge_cooldown_ticks: int = 0

# Tracks weak-attack cooldown (counted alongside the attacking_weak state — by
# the time we're back in idle, this still has a few ticks left to gate spam).
var weak_cooldown_ticks: int = 0

# True while we're in the invincibility portion of dodging.
var dodge_invincible: bool = false

# --- Riposte & combo -----------------------------------------------------
# Active parry arms a riposte: while riposte_ready_ticks > 0, the next damaging
# hit is multiplied by Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier, then consumed.
var riposte_ready_ticks: int = 0
# Combo = consecutive damaging hits; resets on getting hit or on timeout.
var combo: int = 0
var combo_timer_ticks: int = 0
var max_combo: int = 0


func _ready() -> void:
	sprite = _make_sprite(SPRITE_SHEET)
	add_child(sprite)
	# weapon_sprite added after body → drawn on top in same _draw pass.
	weapon_sprite = _make_sprite(WEAPON_SHEET)
	add_child(weapon_sprite)
	set_state(&"idle", 0)
	_update_sprite()


func _make_sprite(sheet: Texture2D) -> Sprite2D:
	var s := Sprite2D.new()
	s.texture = sheet
	s.region_enabled = true
	s.region_rect = Rect2(0, 0, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
	s.scale = Vector2(SPRITE_SCALE, SPRITE_SCALE)
	s.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
	return s


# Called by fight.gd in tick order BEFORE input handling each tick so countdown
# advances first. Input handling lives separately in handle_input().
func tick() -> void:
	swing_this_tick = false
	if dodge_cooldown_ticks > 0:
		dodge_cooldown_ticks -= 1
	if weak_cooldown_ticks > 0:
		weak_cooldown_ticks -= 1

	# Riposte window expires on its own; sprite is refreshed at end of tick.
	if riposte_ready_ticks > 0:
		riposte_ready_ticks -= 1

	# Combo decays if no damaging hit lands within combo_timeout_ticks.
	if combo_timer_ticks > 0:
		combo_timer_ticks -= 1
		if combo_timer_ticks == 0:
			combo = 0

	# Attack states use a separate windup counter so we can fire the swing
	# event on the last windup tick, then continue into recovery within the
	# same `attacking_*` state.
	if state == &"attacking_weak" or state == &"attacking_strong":
		if windup_ticks_remaining > 0:
			windup_ticks_remaining -= 1
			if windup_ticks_remaining == 0:
				# This tick IS the last windup tick — swing now.
				swing_this_tick = true

	# Track block age for parry window — only the first block_active_ticks of a
	# block drain enemy fatigue.
	if state == &"blocking":
		blocking_ticks_elapsed += 1

	# Update dodge phase tracking before generic countdown.
	if state == &"dodging":
		# Invincibility lasts the first dodge_invincibility ticks of the
		# 8-tick state. state_ticks_remaining starts at 8 and decrements.
		var elapsed: int = Balance.dodge_invincibility + Balance.dodge_recover - state_ticks_remaining
		dodge_invincible = elapsed < Balance.dodge_invincibility

	super.tick()

	if state_expired_this_tick:
		_on_state_expired()

	_update_sprite()


func _on_state_expired() -> void:
	match state:
		&"attacking_weak", &"attacking_strong", &"dodging":
			dodge_invincible = false
			set_state(&"idle", 0)
		_:
			pass


# Called from fight.gd._on_tick() AFTER tick() so input is sampled at tick
# boundaries (not per-frame). This keeps everything deterministic on the tick
# grid.
func handle_input() -> void:
	# Inputs are only accepted in idle or blocking states. attacking_* and
	# dodging swallow input until they finish.
	# v1 keyboard-only mapping: arrow keys. Right = fast weak attack, Up =
	# strong attack, Down (hold) = block, Left (tap) = dodge.
	match state:
		&"idle":
			# Block has highest priority (held) — must check before tap actions
			# so player can transition out of attacks-finished into block on
			# the same tick.
			if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_DOWN):
				set_state(&"blocking", 0)
				blocking_ticks_elapsed = 0
				_update_sprite()
				return
			if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_RIGHT) and weak_cooldown_ticks == 0:
				_begin_attack(&"weak")
				return
			if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_UP):
				_begin_attack(&"strong")
				return
			if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_LEFT) and dodge_cooldown_ticks == 0:
				_begin_dodge()
				return
		&"blocking":
			if not Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_DOWN):
				set_state(&"idle", 0)
				_update_sprite()


func _begin_attack(kind: StringName) -> void:
	pending_attack_kind = kind
	var windup: int
	var recover: int
	if kind == &"weak":
		windup = Balance.attack_weak_windup
		recover = Balance.attack_weak_recover
		set_state(&"attacking_weak", windup + recover)
		weak_cooldown_ticks = Balance.attack_weak_cooldown
	else:
		windup = Balance.attack_strong_windup
		recover = Balance.attack_strong_recover
		set_state(&"attacking_strong", windup + recover)
	windup_ticks_remaining = windup

	# Zero-windup attacks (weak) swing instantly this tick.
	if windup == 0:
		swing_this_tick = true
	_update_sprite()


func _begin_dodge() -> void:
	set_state(&"dodging", Balance.dodge_invincibility + Balance.dodge_recover)
	dodge_invincible = true
	dodge_cooldown_ticks = Balance.dodge_cooldown
	_update_sprite()


func is_active_block() -> bool:
	return state == &"blocking" and blocking_ticks_elapsed < Balance.block_active_ticks


func active_block_ticks_remaining() -> int:
	if state != &"blocking":
		return 0
	return maxi(0, Balance.block_active_ticks - blocking_ticks_elapsed)


# --- Riposte -------------------------------------------------------------

func set_riposte_ready() -> void:
	riposte_ready_ticks = Balance.riposte_window_ticks
	_update_sprite()


func is_riposte_ready() -> bool:
	return riposte_ready_ticks > 0


func consume_riposte() -> void:
	riposte_ready_ticks = 0
	_update_sprite()


# Perfect parry = block was started within the last perfect_parry_ticks ticks
# at the moment the weak swing resolves (strict subset of active parry).
func is_perfect_parry() -> bool:
	return state == &"blocking" and blocking_ticks_elapsed <= Balance.perfect_parry_ticks


# --- Combo & rank --------------------------------------------------------

func add_combo() -> void:
	combo += 1
	max_combo = maxi(max_combo, combo)
	combo_timer_ticks = Balance.combo_timeout_ticks


func reset_combo() -> void:
	combo = 0
	combo_timer_ticks = 0


func combo_rank() -> StringName:
	if combo >= 15:
		return &"S"
	elif combo >= 10:
		return &"A"
	elif combo >= 5:
		return &"B"
	elif combo >= 1:
		return &"C"
	return &""


# --- Sprite helpers ------------------------------------------------------

func _current_visual_stance() -> StringName:
	match state:
		&"attacking_strong":
			return &"attacking_strong_windup" if windup_ticks_remaining > 0 else &"attacking_strong_recovery"
		&"blocking":
			return &"blocking_active" if is_active_block() else &"blocking_passive"
		_:
			return state


func _update_sprite() -> void:
	var idx: int = SPRITE_INDEX.get(_current_visual_stance(), 0)
	var rect := Rect2(idx * SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, 0,
			SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
	# Modulate priority: hit_flash > riposte-ready > WHITE.
	var mod: Color
	if hit_flash_ticks > 0:
		mod = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE
	elif riposte_ready_ticks > 0:
		mod = SPRITE_RIPOSTE_MODULATE
	else:
		mod = Color.WHITE
	sprite.region_rect = rect
	sprite.modulate = mod
	weapon_sprite.region_rect = rect
	weapon_sprite.modulate = mod

content.content:
1	class_name Enemy
2	extends Combatant
3	
4	# Abstract enemy. Concrete subclasses must override _pick_attack and
5	# _pick_idle_duration. Owns enemy FSM transitions (idle -> threat -> recovery
6	# -> idle, plus exhausted side branch). Swing event happens on the last tick
7	# of threat_* — fight.gd reads `swing_this_tick` & `pending_attack_kind`.
8	
9	# --- Sprite rendering ----------------------------------------------------
10	# Enemy body = Sprite2D with region_rect cell-picker. Each FSM stance
11	# maps 1:1 to a cell in the strip (no sub-stances for basic enemy). A second
12	# Sprite2D (weapon) overlays the body using the same cell index. These
13	# consts live in the base class for this iteration because we only have one
14	# enemy type (enemy_basic). If a future enemy type wants its own sheets, move
15	# these into the subclass and let base expose abstract accessors.
16	# See sprites/SPRITES.md for the per-cell stance spec.
17	const SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic.png")
18	const WEAPON_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic_weapon.png")
19	const SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(32, 32)
20	const SPRITE_SCALE := 8.0
21	const SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE := Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)
22	const SPRITE_INDEX := {
23		&"idle": 0,
24		&"threat_weak": 1,
25		&"threat_strong": 2,
26		&"recovery_weak": 3,
27		&"recovery_strong": 4,
28		&"exhausted": 5,
29		&"staggered": 5,  # reuse exhausted cell, distinguished by cyan tint
30	}
31	
32	# Readability tints + execute (modulate-only, no new cells). Priority order
33	# (highest first): hit_flash > staggered > threat pulse > execute > white.
34	const THREAT_PULSE_TICKS := 2
35	const STAGGER_MODULATE := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
36	const THREAT_PULSE_MODULATE := Color(1.7, 1.5, 0.5, 1.0)
37	const EXECUTE_MODULATE := Color(1.35, 0.55, 0.55, 1.0)
38	
39	# Floating HP + fatigue bars above the body. Drawn here in _draw() (the sprite
40	# child node draws separately and sits visually below — bars are above body
41	# anyway at y_offset=-82/-70 so there's no overlap).
42	const HP_BAR_SIZE := Vector2(120, 10)
43	const HP_BAR_OFFSET_Y := -150.0  # bar top-left.y, relative to enemy center
44	const FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE := Vector2(120, 6)
45	const FATIGUE_BAR_OFFSET_Y := -138.0  # sits 2 px below HP bar
46	const COLOR_BAR_BG := Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.2, 1.0)
47	const COLOR_HP_BAR_FILL := Color(0.85, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0)
48	const COLOR_FATIGUE_BAR_FILL := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.25, 1.0)
49	
50	var rng := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
51	var sprite: Sprite2D
52	var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D
53	
54	# Set true for exactly one tick when threat phase resolves into a swing.
55	var swing_this_tick: bool = false
56	# Which attack was telegraphed — &"weak" or &"strong" — read on swing tick.
57	var pending_attack_kind: StringName = &"weak"
58	# Set true when the current weak threat is a feint — telegraphs identically but
59	# does NOT swing at the resolve tick (snaps to a short idle instead).
60	var is_feint: bool = false
61	# Counts down from THREAT_PULSE_TICKS so the sprite flashes bright yellow on
62	# threat onset (readability cue). Pure presentation.
63	var threat_pulse_ticks: int = 0
64	
65	
66	func _ready() -> void:
67		rng.randomize()
68		sprite = _make_sprite(SPRITE_SHEET)
69		add_child(sprite)
70		# weapon_sprite added after body → drawn on top in same _draw pass.
71		weapon_sprite = _make_sprite(WEAPON_SHEET)
72		add_child(weapon_sprite)
73		# Start idle.
74		_enter_idle()
75	
76	
77	func _make_sprite(sheet: Texture2D) -> Sprite2D:
78		var s := Sprite2D.new()
79		s.texture = sheet
80		s.region_enabled = true
81		s.region_rect = Rect2(0, 0, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
82		s.scale = Vector2(SPRITE_SCALE, SPRITE_SCALE)
83		s.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
84		return s
85	
86	
87	func _draw() -> void:
88		# Floating HP bar above the enemy body.
89		var hp_origin := Vector2(-HP_BAR_SIZE.x * 0.5, HP_BAR_OFFSET_Y)
90		draw_rect(Rect2(hp_origin, HP_BAR_SIZE), COLOR_BAR_BG)
91		var hp_pct: float = float(hp) / float(Balance.hp_max)
92		draw_rect(Rect2(hp_origin, Vector2(HP_BAR_SIZE.x * hp_pct, HP_BAR_SIZE.y)),
93				COLOR_HP_BAR_FILL)
94	
95		# Floating fatigue bar, just below the HP bar.
96		var fat_origin := Vector2(-FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.x * 0.5, FATIGUE_BAR_OFFSET_Y)
97		draw_rect(Rect2(fat_origin, FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE), COLOR_BAR_BG)
98		var fat_pct: float = float(fatigue) / float(Balance.fatigue_max)
99		draw_rect(Rect2(fat_origin, Vector2(FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.x * fat_pct, FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.y)),
100				COLOR_FATIGUE_BAR_FILL)
101	
102	
103	func tick() -> void:
104		swing_this_tick = false
105		if threat_pulse_ticks > 0:
106			threat_pulse_ticks -= 1
107		super.tick()
108		if state_expired_this_tick:
109			_advance_state_after_expiry()
110		elif (state == &"threat_weak" or state == &"threat_strong") and state_ticks_remaining == 0 and not is_feint:
111			# Defensive: if countdown landed exactly on zero before expiry flag
112			# (shouldn't happen since tick() sets the flag, but keep safe).
113			# Feints never swing — guarded by `not is_feint`.
114			swing_this_tick = true
115		_update_sprite()
116	
117	
118	func _advance_state_after_expiry() -> void:
119		# Called the same tick the previous state expired.
120		match state:
121			&"idle":
122				_decide_attack()
123			&"threat_weak":
124				if is_feint:
125					# Feint: telegraphed but never swings. Snap to a short idle
126					# (feint recovery) and re-decide on expiry.
127					is_feint = false
128					set_state(&"idle", _feint_idle_ticks())
129				else:
130					# Last tick of threat = swing event.
131					swing_this_tick = true
132					set_state(&"recovery_weak", _recovery_weak_ticks())
133			&"threat_strong":
134				swing_this_tick = true
135				set_state(&"recovery_strong", _recovery_strong_ticks())
136			&"recovery_weak", &"recovery_strong":
137				_enter_idle()
138			&"staggered":
139				_enter_idle()
140			&"exhausted":
141				fatigue = Balance.fatigue_max
142				_enter_idle()
143			_:
144				_enter_idle()
145	
146	
147	func _enter_idle() -> void:
148		set_state(&"idle", _pick_idle_duration(rng))
149		pending_attack_kind = &"weak"
150		is_feint = false  # safety: never carry a feint flag across an idle
151	
152	
153	# Perfect-parried by the player → wide-open, cannot act, all hits land.
154	func enter_stagger() -> void:
155		is_feint = false
156		set_state(&"staggered", _stagger_ticks())
157	
158	
159	func _decide_attack() -> void:
160		pending_attack_kind = _pick_attack(rng)
161		# Strong attacks never feint; only weak threats can be a feint.
162		is_feint = (pending_attack_kind == &"weak" and rng.randf() < _feint_probability())
163		if pending_attack_kind == &"strong":
164			var t: int = _threat_strong_ticks()
165			set_state(&"threat_strong", t)
166		else:
167			var tw: int = _threat_weak_ticks()
168			set_state(&"threat_weak", tw)
169		threat_pulse_ticks = THREAT_PULSE_TICKS
170	
171	
172	# Called by fight.gd when fatigue drops to 0 outside of exhausted.
173	func enter_exhausted() -> void:
174		set_state(&"exhausted", _exhausted_ticks())
175	
176	
177	# --- Sprite helpers ------------------------------------------------------
178	
179	func _update_sprite() -> void:
180		# Enemy has no sub-stances — state maps 1:1 to a cell index.
181		var idx: int = SPRITE_INDEX.get(state, 0)
182		var rect := Rect2(idx * SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, 0,
183				SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
184		# Modulate priority (highest first): hit_flash > staggered > threat pulse
185		# > execute > white.
186		var mod: Color
187		if hit_flash_ticks > 0:
188			mod = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE
189		elif state == &"staggered":
190			mod = STAGGER_MODULATE
191		elif threat_pulse_ticks > 0:
192			mod = THREAT_PULSE_MODULATE
193		elif hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp:
194			mod = EXECUTE_MODULATE
195		else:
196			mod = Color.WHITE
197		sprite.region_rect = rect
198		sprite.modulate = mod
199		weapon_sprite.region_rect = rect
200		weapon_sprite.modulate = mod
201	
202	
203	# --- Abstract hooks ------------------------------------------------------
204	
205	func _pick_attack(_rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> StringName:
206		# Override in subclass.
207		return &"weak"
208	
209	
210	func _pick_idle_duration(_rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> int:
211		# Override in subclass.
212		return 48
213	
214	
215	# --- Tunable accessors (overridable) -------------------------------------
216	
217	func _threat_weak_ticks() -> int:
218		return 24
219	
220	
221	func _threat_strong_ticks() -> int:
222		return 32
223	
224	
225	func _recovery_weak_ticks() -> int:
226		return 8
227	
228	
229	func _recovery_strong_ticks() -> int:
230		return 16
231	
232	
233	func _exhausted_ticks() -> int:
234		return 64
235	
236	
237	func _stagger_ticks() -> int:
238		return 12
239	
240	
241	func _feint_probability() -> float:
242		return 0.0
243	
244	
245	func _feint_idle_ticks() -> int:
246		return 6

class_name Enemy
extends Combatant

# Abstract enemy. Concrete subclasses must override _pick_attack and
# _pick_idle_duration. Owns enemy FSM transitions (idle -> threat -> recovery
# -> idle, plus exhausted side branch). Swing event happens on the last tick
# of threat_* — fight.gd reads `swing_this_tick` & `pending_attack_kind`.

# --- Sprite rendering ----------------------------------------------------
# Enemy body = Sprite2D with region_rect cell-picker. Each FSM stance
# maps 1:1 to a cell in the strip (no sub-stances for basic enemy). A second
# Sprite2D (weapon) overlays the body using the same cell index. These
# consts live in the base class for this iteration because we only have one
# enemy type (enemy_basic). If a future enemy type wants its own sheets, move
# these into the subclass and let base expose abstract accessors.
# See sprites/SPRITES.md for the per-cell stance spec.
const SPRITE_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic.png")
const WEAPON_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic_weapon.png")
const SPRITE_CELL_SIZE := Vector2i(32, 32)
const SPRITE_SCALE := 8.0
const SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE := Color(2.5, 2.5, 2.5, 1.0)
const SPRITE_INDEX := {
	&"idle": 0,
	&"threat_weak": 1,
	&"threat_strong": 2,
	&"recovery_weak": 3,
	&"recovery_strong": 4,
	&"exhausted": 5,
	&"staggered": 5,  # reuse exhausted cell, distinguished by cyan tint
}

# Readability tints + execute (modulate-only, no new cells). Priority order
# (highest first): hit_flash > staggered > threat pulse > execute > white.
const THREAT_PULSE_TICKS := 2
const STAGGER_MODULATE := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
const THREAT_PULSE_MODULATE := Color(1.7, 1.5, 0.5, 1.0)
const EXECUTE_MODULATE := Color(1.35, 0.55, 0.55, 1.0)

# Floating HP + fatigue bars above the body. Drawn here in _draw() (the sprite
# child node draws separately and sits visually below — bars are above body
# anyway at y_offset=-82/-70 so there's no overlap).
const HP_BAR_SIZE := Vector2(120, 10)
const HP_BAR_OFFSET_Y := -150.0  # bar top-left.y, relative to enemy center
const FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE := Vector2(120, 6)
const FATIGUE_BAR_OFFSET_Y := -138.0  # sits 2 px below HP bar
const COLOR_BAR_BG := Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.2, 1.0)
const COLOR_HP_BAR_FILL := Color(0.85, 0.2, 0.2, 1.0)
const COLOR_FATIGUE_BAR_FILL := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.25, 1.0)

var rng := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
var sprite: Sprite2D
var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D

# Set true for exactly one tick when threat phase resolves into a swing.
var swing_this_tick: bool = false
# Which attack was telegraphed — &"weak" or &"strong" — read on swing tick.
var pending_attack_kind: StringName = &"weak"
# Set true when the current weak threat is a feint — telegraphs identically but
# does NOT swing at the resolve tick (snaps to a short idle instead).
var is_feint: bool = false
# Counts down from THREAT_PULSE_TICKS so the sprite flashes bright yellow on
# threat onset (readability cue). Pure presentation.
var threat_pulse_ticks: int = 0


func _ready() -> void:
	rng.randomize()
	sprite = _make_sprite(SPRITE_SHEET)
	add_child(sprite)
	# weapon_sprite added after body → drawn on top in same _draw pass.
	weapon_sprite = _make_sprite(WEAPON_SHEET)
	add_child(weapon_sprite)
	# Start idle.
	_enter_idle()


func _make_sprite(sheet: Texture2D) -> Sprite2D:
	var s := Sprite2D.new()
	s.texture = sheet
	s.region_enabled = true
	s.region_rect = Rect2(0, 0, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
	s.scale = Vector2(SPRITE_SCALE, SPRITE_SCALE)
	s.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
	return s


func _draw() -> void:
	# Floating HP bar above the enemy body.
	var hp_origin := Vector2(-HP_BAR_SIZE.x * 0.5, HP_BAR_OFFSET_Y)
	draw_rect(Rect2(hp_origin, HP_BAR_SIZE), COLOR_BAR_BG)
	var hp_pct: float = float(hp) / float(Balance.hp_max)
	draw_rect(Rect2(hp_origin, Vector2(HP_BAR_SIZE.x * hp_pct, HP_BAR_SIZE.y)),
			COLOR_HP_BAR_FILL)

	# Floating fatigue bar, just below the HP bar.
	var fat_origin := Vector2(-FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.x * 0.5, FATIGUE_BAR_OFFSET_Y)
	draw_rect(Rect2(fat_origin, FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE), COLOR_BAR_BG)
	var fat_pct: float = float(fatigue) / float(Balance.fatigue_max)
	draw_rect(Rect2(fat_origin, Vector2(FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.x * fat_pct, FATIGUE_BAR_SIZE.y)),
			COLOR_FATIGUE_BAR_FILL)


func tick() -> void:
	swing_this_tick = false
	if threat_pulse_ticks > 0:
		threat_pulse_ticks -= 1
	super.tick()
	if state_expired_this_tick:
		_advance_state_after_expiry()
	elif (state == &"threat_weak" or state == &"threat_strong") and state_ticks_remaining == 0 and not is_feint:
		# Defensive: if countdown landed exactly on zero before expiry flag
		# (shouldn't happen since tick() sets the flag, but keep safe).
		# Feints never swing — guarded by `not is_feint`.
		swing_this_tick = true
	_update_sprite()


func _advance_state_after_expiry() -> void:
	# Called the same tick the previous state expired.
	match state:
		&"idle":
			_decide_attack()
		&"threat_weak":
			if is_feint:
				# Feint: telegraphed but never swings. Snap to a short idle
				# (feint recovery) and re-decide on expiry.
				is_feint = false
				set_state(&"idle", _feint_idle_ticks())
			else:
				# Last tick of threat = swing event.
				swing_this_tick = true
				set_state(&"recovery_weak", _recovery_weak_ticks())
		&"threat_strong":
			swing_this_tick = true
			set_state(&"recovery_strong", _recovery_strong_ticks())
		&"recovery_weak", &"recovery_strong":
			_enter_idle()
		&"staggered":
			_enter_idle()
		&"exhausted":
			fatigue = Balance.fatigue_max
			_enter_idle()
		_:
			_enter_idle()


func _enter_idle() -> void:
	set_state(&"idle", _pick_idle_duration(rng))
	pending_attack_kind = &"weak"
	is_feint = false  # safety: never carry a feint flag across an idle


# Perfect-parried by the player → wide-open, cannot act, all hits land.
func enter_stagger() -> void:
	is_feint = false
	set_state(&"staggered", _stagger_ticks())


func _decide_attack() -> void:
	pending_attack_kind = _pick_attack(rng)
	# Strong attacks never feint; only weak threats can be a feint.
	is_feint = (pending_attack_kind == &"weak" and rng.randf() < _feint_probability())
	if pending_attack_kind == &"strong":
		var t: int = _threat_strong_ticks()
		set_state(&"threat_strong", t)
	else:
		var tw: int = _threat_weak_ticks()
		set_state(&"threat_weak", tw)
	threat_pulse_ticks = THREAT_PULSE_TICKS


# Called by fight.gd when fatigue drops to 0 outside of exhausted.
func enter_exhausted() -> void:
	set_state(&"exhausted", _exhausted_ticks())


# --- Sprite helpers ------------------------------------------------------

func _update_sprite() -> void:
	# Enemy has no sub-stances — state maps 1:1 to a cell index.
	var idx: int = SPRITE_INDEX.get(state, 0)
	var rect := Rect2(idx * SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, 0,
			SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.x, SPRITE_CELL_SIZE.y)
	# Modulate priority (highest first): hit_flash > staggered > threat pulse
	# > execute > white.
	var mod: Color
	if hit_flash_ticks > 0:
		mod = SPRITE_HIT_FLASH_MODULATE
	elif state == &"staggered":
		mod = STAGGER_MODULATE
	elif threat_pulse_ticks > 0:
		mod = THREAT_PULSE_MODULATE
	elif hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp:
		mod = EXECUTE_MODULATE
	else:
		mod = Color.WHITE
	sprite.region_rect = rect
	sprite.modulate = mod
	weapon_sprite.region_rect = rect
	weapon_sprite.modulate = mod


# --- Abstract hooks ------------------------------------------------------

func _pick_attack(_rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> StringName:
	# Override in subclass.
	return &"weak"


func _pick_idle_duration(_rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> int:
	# Override in subclass.
	return 48


# --- Tunable accessors (overridable) -------------------------------------

func _threat_weak_ticks() -> int:
	return 24


func _threat_strong_ticks() -> int:
	return 32


func _recovery_weak_ticks() -> int:
	return 8


func _recovery_strong_ticks() -> int:
	return 16


func _exhausted_ticks() -> int:
	return 64


func _stagger_ticks() -> int:
	return 12


func _feint_probability() -> float:
	return 0.0


func _feint_idle_ticks() -> int:
	return 6
