attachment.content:
- maestri: Send messages to connected AI agents on the Maestri canvas and get their responses. Also read and write connected sticky notes. Use when the user's intent is to collaborate with another agent on the canvas. Look for actions like 'ask [name] to...', 'tell [name] to...', 'check on [name]', or 'create/update a note'.
- maestri-manager: Create new connected agent terminals on the Maestri canvas and assign them roles. Use when the user asks to assemble a team, delegate parallel work, or spin up additional agents or terminals to help, or to invoke Maestro Mode.
- maestri-portal: Use a browser portal on the Maestri canvas to navigate the web, interact with pages, fill forms, and inspect content. Use when the user asks to browse a URL, test a web UI, or interact with a website.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'glamdring-endless-weakenemy-impl',
  description: 'Implement Endless mode + fatigue-less weak enemy (skeleton, chip-through-block) across Glamdring, one agent per disjoint file set against a frozen spec',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Implement', detail: 'parallel file-owner agents implement their file(s) against the shared spec + public contract' },
  ],
}

const REPO = '/Users/marekpalavsky/godot/glamdring'

// FROZEN SPEC — no backticks anywhere (template literal). Cross-file names fixed
// by the PUBLIC CONTRACT so parallel owners implement identical interfaces.
const SPEC = `
GLAMDRING — "ENDLESS + WEAK ENEMY" UPDATE
Game: 1v1 reaction combat, Godot 4.6, fixed 16 ticks/sec, player invincible. All gameplay
logic in fight.gd _on_tick(). Tunables in balance.json via the Balance autoload. Sprites =
one 32x32 cell per stance, region_rect swap, modulate tints, no new art. Existing features
that MUST keep working: fatigue->exhausted (basic enemy), active/perfect parry, riposte
(x1.6 next damaging hit), staggered state, combo+rank (C/B/A/S at 1/5/10/15), enemy feints,
execute (<=25hp -> x1.5 + red tint), hit-stop, threat pulse. Comments may mix Czech+English.

================ THE TWO FEATURES ================

F1. ENDLESS MODE (new default loop)
  - No more win screen. When enemy hp <= 0: score += kill bonus, wave += 1, immediately
    spawn the next enemy at the same position (456, 384), show a fading "WAVE N" banner.
  - Wave composition: waves 1-2 = weak enemy (new type below); waves 3-4 = enemy_basic;
    waves 5+ alternate basic/weak (odd wave -> basic, even wave -> weak) with MILD scaling
    applied to the BASIC enemy only (speed_scale shortens its telegraphs/recovery toward
    floors; small strong-probability bonus, capped). Weak enemy never scales.
  - Combo PERSISTS across waves (resets only on player getting hit or combo timeout).
  - Space restarts the whole run anytime (reload_current_scene), polled in the live tick.
  - SCORE = sum of damage actually dealt (chip + clean, final rounded ints) + kill bonus of
    (endless_kill_score_per_wave * wave) per kill.
  - HUD: WAVE + SCORE + max combo readout top-left; combo+rank stays top-center; the old
    VYHRA win overlay is REMOVED entirely, replaced by the transient WAVE banner.

F2. WEAK ENEMY (skeleton) — fatigue-less, chip-through-block, in new file enemy_weak.gd
  - extends Enemy. Sprite body sheet: res://sprites/skeleton-basic.png (192x32, 6 cells,
    identical layout to enemy_basic; currently a placeholder copy — note in comment).
    Weapon sheet: reuse res://sprites/enemy_basic_weapon.png (art TODO comment).
    Distinguishing visual until real art exists: bony base tint (see _base_tint hook).
  - NO fatigue: no fatigue bar drawn, never enters exhausted, no fatigue drains anywhere,
    and NO HP/fatigue regen when it hits the player (a player miss just wastes the turn).
  - CHIP: player hits while it is in idle deal REDUCED damage straight to HP:
    chip dmg = int(round(base_damage * chip_multiplier)) where base is hp_damage_weak (15)
    or hp_damage_strong (25); chip_multiplier ~0.34 -> 5 / 8 dmg. Chip hits: count toward
    SCORE, but do NOT build combo, do NOT consume riposte, do NOT get riposte/execute
    multipliers; light fx only (small gray-blue particle burst + hitstop_weak; NO hit flash).
  - FULL damage in recovery_weak/recovery_strong/staggered (riposte/execute multipliers and
    combo apply exactly as for the basic enemy). threat_* still immune.
  - Parry interactions stay: active parry vs its weak swing still arms riposte; perfect
    parry still staggers it. Only the fatigue DRAIN part disappears.
  - Weaker stats: max_hp 70, long readable telegraphs, generous recovery, mostly weak
    attacks (strong_probability 0.05), NO feints.

================ NEW balance.json SECTIONS + balance.gd ================

Add to balance.json (keep all existing keys/sections untouched):

  "enemy_weak": {
    "hp_max": 70, "chip_multiplier": 0.34,
    "threat_weak_ticks": 14, "threat_strong_ticks": 18,
    "recovery_weak_ticks": 14, "recovery_strong_ticks": 22,
    "idle_min_ticks": 24, "idle_max_ticks": 56,
    "strong_probability": 0.05, "stagger_ticks": 18, "exhausted_ticks": 48
  },
  "endless": {
    "scale_step": 0.06, "scale_cap": 1.5, "strong_prob_bonus_cap": 0.15,
    "threat_weak_floor": 4, "threat_strong_floor": 6,
    "recovery_weak_floor": 2, "recovery_strong_floor": 4,
    "banner_ticks": 24, "kill_score_per_wave": 50
  }

balance.gd:
  - Add: var enemy_weak: Dictionary = {}        # per-type tunables read by enemy_weak.gd
    Loaded in _load_balance() as: enemy_weak = data.get("enemy_weak", {}) as Dictionary
    Do NOT flatten enemy_weak into top-level vars (would collide with enemy_basic flats).
  - Add flat endless vars with defaults mirroring the JSON, parsed from an "endless" dict
    (same int()/float() pattern as the existing sections):
      var endless_scale_step: float = 0.06
      var endless_scale_cap: float = 1.5
      var endless_strong_prob_bonus_cap: float = 0.15
      var endless_threat_weak_floor: int = 4
      var endless_threat_strong_floor: int = 6
      var endless_recovery_weak_floor: int = 2
      var endless_recovery_strong_floor: int = 4
      var endless_banner_ticks: int = 24
      var endless_kill_score_per_wave: int = 50

================ PUBLIC CONTRACT (exact names/signatures — DO NOT DEVIATE) ================

combatant.gd adds:
  func max_hp() -> int                      # return Balance.hp_max  (virtual; subclasses override)
  damage_hp/heal_hp clamp with max_hp() instead of Balance.hp_max.

enemy.gd (base Enemy) adds:
  var speed_scale: float = 1.0              # endless scaling knob; 1.0 = unscaled
  var strong_prob_bonus: float = 0.0        # endless additive strong-prob bonus (basic only)
  func has_fatigue() -> bool                # base: return true
  func idle_chip_multiplier() -> float      # base: return 0.0  (0.0 sentinel = full block)
  func _base_tint() -> Color                # base: return Color.WHITE
  func _sprite_sheet() -> Texture2D         # base: return SPRITE_SHEET
  func _weapon_sheet() -> Texture2D         # base: return WEAPON_SHEET
  _ready(): FIRST line is  hp = max_hp()  , then existing body but _make_sprite(_sprite_sheet())
    and _make_sprite(_weapon_sheet()).
  _draw(): hp pct uses float(max_hp()); the fatigue-bar block wrapped in  if has_fatigue():
  _update_sprite(): final else branch assigns  mod = _base_tint()  (instead of Color.WHITE).
    Priority stays: hit_flash > staggered > threat pulse > execute > _base_tint().

enemy_basic.gd changes (scaling with floors; behavior identical at speed_scale 1.0):
  func _pick_attack(rng_): return &"strong" if rng_.randf() < clampf(Balance.strong_probability + strong_prob_bonus, 0.0, 1.0) else &"weak"
  func _threat_weak_ticks() -> int: return maxi(Balance.endless_threat_weak_floor, int(round(Balance.threat_weak_ticks / speed_scale)))
  func _threat_strong_ticks() -> int: return maxi(Balance.endless_threat_strong_floor, int(round(Balance.threat_strong_ticks / speed_scale)))
  func _recovery_weak_ticks() -> int: return maxi(Balance.endless_recovery_weak_floor, int(round(Balance.recovery_weak_ticks / speed_scale)))
  func _recovery_strong_ticks() -> int: return maxi(Balance.endless_recovery_strong_floor, int(round(Balance.recovery_strong_ticks / speed_scale)))
  (idle/exhausted/stagger/feint accessors unchanged.)

enemy_weak.gd (NEW FILE) — extends Enemy:
  # Weak skeleton: no fatigue, chip-through-block, low HP, slow readable attacks, no feints.
  const BODY_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/skeleton-basic.png")     # placeholder copy of enemy_basic.png — real skeleton art TODO
  const WEAPON_SHEET_WEAK := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic_weapon.png")  # reuse — skeleton weapon art TODO
  const BASE_TINT := Color(0.92, 0.95, 0.82, 1.0)   # bony pale tint to tell it apart from basic until real art
  func _sprite_sheet() -> Texture2D: return BODY_SHEET
  func _weapon_sheet() -> Texture2D: return WEAPON_SHEET_WEAK
  func _base_tint() -> Color: return BASE_TINT
  func has_fatigue() -> bool: return false
  func idle_chip_multiplier() -> float: return float(Balance.enemy_weak.get("chip_multiplier", 0.34))
  func max_hp() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("hp_max", 70))
  func _pick_attack(rng_): return &"strong" if rng_.randf() < float(Balance.enemy_weak.get("strong_probability", 0.05)) else &"weak"
  func _pick_idle_duration(rng_): return rng_.randi_range(int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("idle_min_ticks", 24)), int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("idle_max_ticks", 56)))
  func _threat_weak_ticks() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("threat_weak_ticks", 14))
  func _threat_strong_ticks() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("threat_strong_ticks", 18))
  func _recovery_weak_ticks() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("recovery_weak_ticks", 14))
  func _recovery_strong_ticks() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("recovery_strong_ticks", 22))
  func _stagger_ticks() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("stagger_ticks", 18))
  func _exhausted_ticks() -> int: return int(Balance.enemy_weak.get("exhausted_ticks", 48))  # unreachable (no fatigue) but sane
  func _feint_probability() -> float: return 0.0
  (speed_scale deliberately ignored — fixed readable timings.)

hud.gd update() — NEW exact signature (show_win REMOVED, 3 endless params appended):
  func update(p_enemy_hp: int, p_enemy_fatigue: int, p_enemy_state: StringName,
      p_enemy_state_ticks: int, p_player_state: StringName, p_player_state_ticks: int,
      p_dodge_cd: int, p_combo: int, p_combo_rank: StringName, p_max_combo: int,
      p_wave: int, p_score: int, p_banner_ticks: int) -> void

fight.gd must call exactly:
  hud.update(enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
      player.state, p_ticks, player.dodge_cooldown_ticks,
      player.combo, player.combo_rank(), player.max_combo,
      wave, score, banner_ticks)
and uses: enemy.has_fatigue(), enemy.idle_chip_multiplier(), e.speed_scale, e.strong_prob_bonus.

================ PER-FILE IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL ================

--- combatant.gd ---
  Add max_hp() (after trigger_flash or near getters):
      func max_hp() -> int:
          return Balance.hp_max
  damage_hp: hp = clampi(hp - amount, 0, max_hp())
  heal_hp:   hp = clampi(hp + amount, 0, max_hp())
  Leave the var hp declaration as-is (Enemy._ready() sizes it).

--- enemy.gd ---
  Per contract above. Notes:
  - hp = max_hp() must be the FIRST statement of _ready() (virtual max_hp can't run in the
    var initializer; dynamically spawned enemies would otherwise start at 100).
  - Keep SPRITE_SHEET/WEAPON_SHEET consts (base accessors return them).
  - Fatigue bar block in _draw() (the fat_origin..draw_rect lines) wrapped in if has_fatigue():
  - No other behavior changes.

--- enemy_basic.gd ---
  Per contract. speed_scale defaults to 1.0 so round(x/1.0) == x and floors never bind:
  pre-endless behavior is bit-identical. Keep comments style.

--- enemy_weak.gd ---
  New file per contract. Header comment (Czech+EN ok) describing the type: fatigue-less,
  chip-through-block, low HP, slow telegraphs, no feints; placeholder art note.

--- fight.gd + fight.tscn ---
  fight.gd:
  - Add consts near the preloads/colors:
      const ENEMY_BASIC := preload("res://enemy_basic.gd")
      const ENEMY_WEAK := preload("res://enemy_weak.gd")
      const ENEMY_SPAWN_POS := Vector2(456, 384)
      const COLOR_CHIP := Color(0.7, 0.75, 0.85, 1.0)
      const PARTICLES_CHIP := 4
  - Replace var game_over with:
      var wave: int = 0
      var score: int = 0
      var banner_ticks: int = 0
  - Change @onready var enemy: Node2D = $Enemy  ->  var enemy: Node2D
    (the static Enemy node is removed from the scene; spawn is dynamic).
  - Add _ready():
      func _ready() -> void:
          _advance_wave()        # spawns wave 1 + banner
  - New funcs:
      func _advance_wave() -> void:
          wave += 1
          _spawn_enemy(wave)
          banner_ticks = Balance.endless_banner_ticks
      func _spawn_enemy(w: int) -> void:
          # composition: 1-2 weak, 3-4 basic, 5+ alternate (odd basic / even weak)
          var script: GDScript
          if w <= 2: script = ENEMY_WEAK
          elif w <= 4: script = ENEMY_BASIC
          else: script = ENEMY_BASIC if (w % 2 == 1) else ENEMY_WEAK
          var e: Node2D = script.new()
          e.position = ENEMY_SPAWN_POS
          if w >= 5:
              e.speed_scale = clampf(1.0 + float(w - 4) * Balance.endless_scale_step, 1.0, Balance.endless_scale_cap)
              e.strong_prob_bonus = minf(float(w - 4) * 0.01, Balance.endless_strong_prob_bonus_cap)
          if enemy != null and is_instance_valid(enemy):
              enemy.queue_free()
          add_child(e)
          enemy = e
      func _on_enemy_defeated() -> void:
          score += Balance.endless_kill_score_per_wave * wave
          _advance_wave()
  - _on_tick(): REMOVE the whole game_over early-return block. New top of _on_tick():
      # Space restarts the entire run at any time.
      if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):
          get_tree().reload_current_scene()
          return
      if banner_ticks > 0:
          banner_ticks -= 1
    Then the existing tick order unchanged (player.tick, enemy.tick, handle_input, resolve
    enemy swing, resolve player swing). Gate the exhausted transition:
      if enemy.has_fatigue() and enemy.fatigue <= 0 and enemy.state != &"exhausted":
          enemy.enter_exhausted()
    Replace the win check with (MUST stay after both resolves, before _update_hud):
      if enemy.hp <= 0:
          _on_enemy_defeated()
      _update_hud()
  - _resolve_enemy_swing():
      * active parry branch: wrap ONLY the drain in the gate —
          if enemy.has_fatigue():
              enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
        player.set_riposte_ready() and the perfect-parry enter_stagger() path stay UNgated.
      * the player-hit fall-through: wrap BOTH regen lines —
          if enemy.has_fatigue():
              enemy.heal_hp(Balance.enemy_hp_regen_on_hit)
              enemy.add_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit)
        Keep shake/flash/particles/reset_combo/hitstop as-is (player still got hit).
  - _resolve_player_swing():
      * idle branch becomes:
          if es == &"idle":
              var chip: float = enemy.idle_chip_multiplier()
              if chip > 0.0:
                  # Chip-through-block: reduced damage straight to HP. No combo, no
                  # riposte consume, no execute — clean punishes stay the rewarded path.
                  var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
                  var dmg: int = int(round(float(base_dmg) * chip))
                  enemy.damage_hp(dmg)
                  score += dmg
                  _trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
                  _trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)
                  _spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_CHIP, PARTICLES_CHIP)
                  return
              if kind == &"strong":
                  if enemy.has_fatigue():
                      enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle)
                  _trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
                  _spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
              return
      * full-hit branch (recovery/exhausted/staggered): after enemy.damage_hp(int(round(dmg)))
        add  score += int(round(dmg))  . Everything else unchanged.
  - _update_hud(): new hud.update call per contract (game_over arg gone).
  fight.tscn:
  - Remove the [node name="Enemy" ...] block AND its ext_resource line for enemy_basic.gd
    (id "3_enemy"). Keep Player/HUD/Background untouched. Careful: text scene format.

--- hud.gd ---
  - Remove show_win var + the p_show_win param. New signature per contract; store wave,
    score, banner_ticks in new vars.
  - _on_canvas_draw():
      * Top-left readout (small, y around 36..80, font ~20, COLOR_TEXT):
          "WAVE %d" , "SCORE %d" , "MAX COMBO %d [%s]" (rank via existing _rank_for/_rank_color).
      * Keep combo+rank top-center block unchanged.
      * DELETE the whole win-overlay block (overlay rect, VYHRA, Space hint, max combo line).
      * WAVE banner: when banner_ticks > 0 draw centered "WAVE %d" (font ~72) at mid-screen,
        alpha = float(banner_ticks) / float(maxi(1, Balance.endless_banner_ticks)), color
        gold-ish Color(1.0, 0.95, 0.5, alpha). Pure fade-out.
  - Keep all existing debug text + key hints.

--- docs (gameplay.md, plan.md, sprites/SPRITES.md, CLAUDE.md, TODO.md) ---
  gameplay.md:
    - Replace the "Win condition" section with an "Endless mód" section: kill -> wave++ +
      kill bonus (endless_kill_score_per_wave * wave), immediate respawn, WAVE banner, no
      lose condition, Space restarts run anytime, combo persists across waves, score = damage
      dealt + kill bonuses. Wave composition 1-2 weak / 3-4 basic / 5+ alternating + mild
      scaling (floors, caps).
    - New "Enemy: weak (skeleton)" subsection: no fatigue (no exhausted, no regen on hitting
      player, no drains), chip-through-block table (idle hit = chip_multiplier * base, full
      dmg in recovery/staggered, threat immune; chip: no combo/riposte/execute), stats
      (hp 70, slow telegraphs, 5% strong, no feints), parry/stagger/riposte still apply.
    - Update FSM/summary notes accordingly (basic enemy unchanged).
  plan.md:
    - Architektura souborů: add enemy_weak.gd; note enemy spawns dynamically (fight.gd
      _spawn_enemy), static Enemy node removed from fight.tscn.
    - Tunable konstanty: add the enemy_weak + endless JSON blocks (values above) and bullet
      tables; document Balance.enemy_weak as a Dictionary (not flattened) and flat endless_* vars.
    - Document the capability hooks (has_fatigue / idle_chip_multiplier / max_hp /
      _sprite_sheet / _weapon_sheet / _base_tint / speed_scale / strong_prob_bonus).
    - fight.gd description: tick top = Space restart + banner; win check -> _on_enemy_defeated.
    - HUD: new update() signature, top-left wave/score/max-combo, WAVE banner, win overlay gone.
    - Verifikace: replace step 8 (Win) with endless tests: wave advance + banner, chip damage
      (no combo/riposte), weak enemy no-regen/no-fatigue-bar, composition 1-2/3-4/5+ and
      scaling floors, Space restart. Keep other steps.
  sprites/SPRITES.md:
    - Section for skeleton-basic.png (192x32, 6 cells, layout identical to enemy_basic).
      FLAG: currently a byte-identical placeholder copy of enemy_basic.png; weapon overlay
      reuses enemy_basic_weapon.png; distinguishing bony tint via _base_tint(); real skeleton
      body+weapon art TODO.
    - Document the _sprite_sheet()/_weapon_sheet() accessor refactor (the file already
      anticipates it in the extension guide) and _base_tint() in the modulate priority list
      (replaces the WHITE default at the lowest priority).
  CLAUDE.md:
    - Project structure: add enemy_weak.gd line; fight.gd line mentions wave spawning.
    - Conventions: extend the per-type overrides bullet with capability hooks (has_fatigue,
      idle_chip_multiplier, max_hp, sheet accessors, _base_tint) and Balance.enemy_weak dict
      + endless_* flats.
    - Out of scope: remove "Více typů nepřátel" (now in) and update the win/restart mention.
  TODO.md:
    - Tick off "Druhý typ nepřítele" and "Endless waves" (move to the Hotovo section with a
      short note: skeleton = fatigue-less chip enemy; endless = new default).

================ CORRECTNESS NOTES ================
  - Respawn MUST happen at the end of _on_tick (after both resolves) so fx in the same tick
    still reference the dying enemy's position. queue_free old, add_child new, rebind enemy.
  - Never call player.reset_combo() on wave advance — combo persists.
  - hp = max_hp() first line of Enemy._ready() (else spawned weak enemy starts at 100).
  - Floors guarantee telegraphs never reach 0 ticks (a 0-tick threat would fire an unfair
    same-tick swing via the defensive branch in enemy.tick()).
  - GDScript 4.x, tabs for indentation, no physics/Area2D, no AnimationPlayer.
`

const FILE_TASK_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['ownedFiles', 'changesSummary', 'contractConcerns', 'newSymbols', 'done'],
  properties: {
    ownedFiles: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    changesSummary: { type: 'string' },
    newSymbols: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
    contractConcerns: { type: 'string', description: '"" if none' },
    done: { type: 'boolean' },
  },
}

const ASSIGNMENTS = [
  {
    key: 'enemy-hierarchy',
    files: [REPO + '/combatant.gd', REPO + '/enemy.gd', REPO + '/enemy_basic.gd', REPO + '/enemy_weak.gd'],
    task: 'Implement the combatant.gd, enemy.gd, enemy_basic.gd sections and CREATE the new enemy_weak.gd per the contract: max_hp() on Combatant + clamps; capability hooks + speed_scale/strong_prob_bonus + sprite/tint accessors + hp=max_hp() first in _ready() + fatigue-bar gate + _base_tint() in _update_sprite() on enemy.gd; scaled accessors with floors on enemy_basic.gd; the full new enemy_weak.gd file.',
  },
  {
    key: 'fight',
    files: [REPO + '/fight.gd', REPO + '/fight.tscn'],
    task: 'Implement the fight.gd + fight.tscn section: endless state (wave/score/banner_ticks, game_over removed), dynamic spawn (_ready/_advance_wave/_spawn_enemy/_on_enemy_defeated, preloads, ENEMY_SPAWN_POS), Space restart + banner decrement at top of _on_tick, has_fatigue() gates (exhausted check, parry drain only, both regen lines, strong-in-idle drain), the chip branch in the idle case, score accumulation in both damage paths, the new 13-arg hud.update call, and removal of the static Enemy node + its ext_resource from fight.tscn (text scene format — be careful).',
  },
  {
    key: 'hud',
    files: [REPO + '/hud.gd'],
    task: 'Implement the hud.gd section: new 13-arg update() signature (show_win removed; wave/score/banner_ticks added), top-left WAVE/SCORE/MAX COMBO readout, delete the win overlay block, add the fading centered WAVE banner driven by banner_ticks / Balance.endless_banner_ticks. Keep debug text, key hints, and the top-center combo+rank block unchanged.',
  },
  {
    key: 'balance',
    files: [REPO + '/balance.json', REPO + '/balance.gd'],
    task: 'Add the "enemy_weak" and "endless" sections to balance.json (exact keys/values from the spec; keep existing formatting style), and in balance.gd add var enemy_weak: Dictionary = {} (loaded as a dict, NOT flattened) plus the nine flat endless_* vars with defaults, parsed in _load_balance() with the existing int()/float() pattern.',
  },
  {
    key: 'docs',
    files: [REPO + '/gameplay.md', REPO + '/plan.md', REPO + '/sprites/SPRITES.md', REPO + '/CLAUDE.md', REPO + '/TODO.md'],
    task: 'Sync all five docs per the docs section of the spec: gameplay.md (Endless mód section replacing Win condition; weak-enemy subsection with chip table), plan.md (architecture, tunables incl. new JSON blocks, capability hooks, fight flow, HUD, verification steps), SPRITES.md (skeleton sheet + accessors + _base_tint in priority + placeholder-art flag), CLAUDE.md (structure + conventions + out-of-scope updates), TODO.md (tick off the two shipped items). Do NOT edit any .gd/.json/.tscn files. Keep values consistent with the spec defaults.',
  },
]

phase('Implement')
const results = await parallel(ASSIGNMENTS.map(a => () =>
  agent(
    'You are implementing one slice of a frozen, fully-specified feature package for an existing Godot 4.6 game. Read your assigned file(s) FIRST (read every file you own before editing), then apply EXACTLY the edits the spec assigns to your files — no more, no less. Preserve existing code style, TAB indentation, and the Czech+English comment mix. Use Edit/Write to actually modify files. Cross-file names MUST match the PUBLIC CONTRACT verbatim. Return the structured summary when done.\n\n' +
    'YOUR OWNED FILE(S): ' + a.files.join(', ') + '\n' +
    'YOUR TASK: ' + a.task + '\n\n' +
    '================ FULL SPEC ================\n' + SPEC,
    { label: 'impl:' + a.key, phase: 'Implement', schema: FILE_TASK_SCHEMA }
  ).then(r => r ? { key: a.key, ...r } : { key: a.key, failed: true })
))

return results

content.text:
Dobrý nápad — obchod krásně zapadne do endless smyčky (mezi vlnami). Tady je návrh designu; nic zatím neimplementuju (endless + skeleton se právě dodělávají na pozadí, shop by stavěl na nich).

## 💰 Ekonomika

- **Měna: zlato** — oddělené od skóre (skóre zůstane prestiž/leaderboard, zlato se utrácí).
- **Výdělek:**
  - kill: `15 + 5×vlna` zlata
  - bonus za rank comba při killu (C +0, B +5, A +10, S +20)
  - perfect parry: +3 (odměna za skill, ne za grind)
  - „čistá vlna" (bez zásahu): +10
- **Kdy nakupuješ:** po každé N-té vlně (např. po 2.) se otevře **shop overlay** — tick loop stojí, vybereš, pokračuje se. Alternativa: nabídka 3 náhodných položek po každé vlně (roguelite styl) — větší variabilita, menší „shopping list" paralýza.

## 🛒 Co kupovat (hráč je nesmrtelný → vše točím na ofenzivu, tempo a skill-výplaty)

**⚔️ Ofenziva** *(stackovatelné tiery, rostoucí cena, cap)*
| Upgrade | Efekt | Pozn. |
|---|---|---|
| Ostřejší čepel | +3 weak dmg / tier (max 3×) | základní DPS |
| Těžší úder | +5 strong dmg / tier (max 3×) | |
| Mistr riposty | riposte ×1.6 → ×1.8 → ×2.0 | synergie s parry stylem |
| Kat | execute práh 25 → 32 → 40 HP | dřívější finishery |
| Prolamovač | chip multiplier 0.34 → 0.5 | proti skeletonům/gardě |

**⚡ Tempo** *(zrychlují hráče — pozor na cap, ať se nedá spamovat)*
- Rychlé zápěstí — weak cooldown 12t → 10t → 8t
- Lehké nohy — dodge cooldown 24t → 18t → 14t
- Plynulý nápřah — strong windup 6t → 5t

**🛡️ Parry / skill-výplaty** *(nejzajímavější kategorie — zvětšují okna, ne čísla)*
- Šermířský reflex — perfect parry okno 3t → 4t → 5t
- Dlouhá riposta — riposte okno 14t → 20t
- Drtivý parry — stagger trvá 16t → 22t
- Vyčerpávající blok — parry drain fatigue 10 → 15 (rychlejší exhausted u basic)

**🎲 Risk / reward (cursed items — levné/zdarma, ale něco stojí)**
- Krvavá smlouva: +30 % dmg, ale enemy telegrafy −20 % (rychlejší)
- Chamtivost: +50 % zlata, ale combo timeout −50 %
- Berserk: execute ×2.0, ale chip damage 0 (garde neprorazíš vůbec)

**🧪 Consumables (jednorázovky na běžící vlnu)**
- Dýmovnice — okamžitý stagger enemy
- Válečný roh — další zásah je auto-crit ×2
- Brousek — +50 % dmg jen na tuhle vlnu

## 🏗️ Jak by to sedlo do architektury

- Základní hodnoty zůstávají v `balance.json`; upgrady jsou **runtime modifikátory** (offsety/multipliery) v novém singletonu/dictu `Upgrades`, který resolve funkce ve `fight.gd` čtou přes helper (`upgraded_weak_damage()` apod.). Reset při restartu runu (Space) — žádná persistence napříč runy (v1).
- Shop = `CanvasLayer` overlay + nový „shopping" stav ve fight loopu (ticky stojí), ovládání šipkami + Space/Enter na koupi. Ceny/efekty v `balance.json` sekci `shop`.

**Moje doporučení pro v1:** roguelite varianta — po každé 2. vlně nabídka **3 náhodných karet** (kup 1 nebo skip za +zlato), kategorie Ofenziva + Parry + občas cursed. Je to nejmíň UI práce (žádný scrollovací obchod) a největší replayabilita.

Řekni, který směr se ti líbí (plný obchod vs. 3 náhodné karty, a jestli chceš cursed items / consumables), a až doběhne endless implementace, naplánuju to.

attachment.snippet:
1	# CLAUDE.md — Glamdring
2	
3	Reaction-combat 1v1 hra v Godot 4.6 Mobile profilu. Player (stance sprite vlevo, zelené tóny) vs Enemy (stance sprite vpravo, šedo-červené tóny). Pevný timestep **16 ticků/s**.
4	Pro implementace pouzivej sub agenty at je hlavni context cistsi.
5	
6	## Autoritativní specifikace
7	
8	- **`gameplay.md`** — pravidla, FSM stavy, timingy, balance, swing resolve.
9	- **`plan.md`** — architektura souborů, tunable konstanty, stance sprite rendering, vizuální layout, verifikační scénáře.
10	- **`sprites/SPRITES.md`** — per-character spritesheet layout (cell index → stance).
11	
12	Před netriviální změnou si přečti oba soubory. Pokud upravuješ pravidla/timing, **synchronizuj s gameplay.md i plan.md** (linter to nedělá).
13	
14	## Project structure
15	
16	```
17	fight.tscn / fight.gd        — kořenová scéna + tick loop, swing resolves, HUD update, endless
18	                               waves (dynamický spawn enemy per wave — _spawn_enemy, score, banner)
19	combatant.gd                 — class_name Combatant (base: hp, fatigue, state, state_ticks_remaining,
20	                               virtual max_hp())
21	enemy.gd                     — class_name Enemy extends Combatant (FSM transitions, abstract +
22	                               capability hooks, endless knoby speed_scale/strong_prob_bonus)
23	enemy_basic.gd               — extends Enemy (per-type tunable konstanty, endless škálování s floory)
24	enemy_weak.gd                — extends Enemy (weak skeleton: bez fatigue, chip-through-block, hp 70)
25	player.gd                    — extends Combatant (input → state transitions)
26	hud.gd                       — CanvasLayer (debug state text, WAVE/SCORE/MAX COMBO readout,
27	                               combo top-center, WAVE banner)
28	balance.json                 — runtime tunables (HP/fatigue caps, ticky, damage, cooldowns, AI prob.,
29	                               sekce enemy_weak + endless)
30	balance.gd                   — autoload singleton `Balance`, načítá balance.json při startu
31	sprites/                     — stance spritesheets (`player.png`, `enemy_basic.png`,
32	                               `skeleton-basic.png` — placeholder) + `SPRITES.md`
33	project.godot                — fight.tscn jako main_scene, 720×1280 portrait, stretch=viewport,
34	                               autoload Balance="*res://balance.gd"
35	```
36	
37	## Conventions
38	
39	- **Tick loop:** akumulátor v `fight.gd._process(delta)`; veškerá herní logika (state countdowny, AI, swing resolve, hit resolve) **výhradně** v `_on_tick()`. Vykreslování přes `queue_redraw()` na konci `_process`.
40	- **Tunable konstanty:** Herní balanc (ticky, damage, cooldowns, HP/fatigue caps, AI probabilities) je v `balance.json` v rootu projektu, načítaný autoloadem `Balance` při startu. Editace JSONu + restart aplikuje změny bez recompilu. Vizuální / animační / architektonické konstanty (`TICKS_PER_SECOND`, `SPRITE_SHEET`, `SPRITE_CELL_SIZE`, `SPRITE_SCALE`, `SPRITE_INDEX`, HP/fatigue bar dimensions, shake/particle hodnoty) zůstávají jako `const` v příslušném skriptu. Per-type AI tunables enemy (`threat_*_ticks`, `recovery_*_ticks`, `idle_*`, `strong_probability`) jsou v sekci `enemy_basic` JSONu.
41	- **Per-type overrides:** `enemy.gd` poskytuje defaults pro `_threat_*_ticks` apod. `enemy_basic.gd` je přepisuje a vrací hodnoty z `Balance.*`. Při ladění hodnot **edituj `balance.json`**, base `enemy.gd` zůstává s hardcoded defaults pro budoucí typy. K tomu base `Enemy` nabízí **capability hooky**: `has_fatigue()` (default `true`), `idle_chip_multiplier()` (default `0.0` = plný blok), `max_hp()` (default `Balance.hp_max`, na `Combatant`), sheet accessory `_sprite_sheet()` / `_weapon_sheet()` a `_base_tint()` (klidový tint, nejnižší modulate priorita) — `enemy_weak.gd` je přepisuje. Tunables weak skeletonu jsou v `Balance.enemy_weak` (**Dictionary**, neflattenuje se — kolize s enemy_basic flaty), endless tunables jako flat `Balance.endless_*` vary.
42	- **No physics:** žádné `PhysicsBody`, `Area2D`, `CollisionShape`. Vše stavové; pokud potřeba kolize, ručně AABB/distance check.
43	- **Stance sprites:** Combatant = body `Sprite2D` + weapon `Sprite2D` overlay (oba s `region_rect` cell-pickerem nad horizontálním sheetem). Weapon sheet má identický layout jako body, takže index se počítá jednou a aplikuje na oba sprity. Každý FSM stance = jedna buňka. `SPRITE_INDEX` dict v každém `.gd` mapuje stance → cell index. Sub-stance (player active/passive block, strong windup/recovery) řeší `_current_visual_stance()`. Žádné inter-pose animace — pouze sprite swap na hranici ticku. Hit flash = `modulate * 2.5` na obě vrstvy. Spec stancí + sheet layouty viz `sprites/SPRITES.md`.
44	- **HUD constants:** bary používají `Balance.hp_max` / `Balance.fatigue_max` pro percenta — pokud změníš cap v `balance.json`, bar se škáluje automaticky.
45	- **Czech:** uživatel píše česky, technické termíny v EN. Komentáře v kódu mohou být kombinované (jak existuje).
46	
47	## Controls (v1 — keyboard only)
48	
49	| Klávesa | Akce |
50	|---|---|
51	| `→` (tap) | Fast weak attack (instant swing, 4t recovery, 12t cooldown, damage 15) |
52	| `↑` | Strong attack (6-tick telegraph + 10-tick recovery) |
53	| `↓` (hold) | Block — prvních 8t "active parry" (drain enemy fatigue při weak útoku) |
54	| `←` (tap) | Dodge (6t i-frames + 2t recover, 24t cooldown) |
55	| `Space` | Restart celého runu (kdykoliv, polled v live ticku) |
56	
57	Touch input pro mobil je odložený TODO (viz plan.md "Mimo scope").
58	
59	## How to verify
60	
61	Otevři projekt v Godot 4.6 editoru, spusť `fight.tscn`. Manuální test scénáře v `plan.md` sekce **Verifikace** — projdi 1–18 (endless/chip testy = 8, 16–18).
62	
63	## Working style
64	
65	- Když uživatel přidá / změní gameplay pravidlo: vždy aktualizuj **kód v glamdring** + **gameplay.md** + **plan.md** současně.
66	- Drobné implementační detaily (vizuál, barvy, anchor pozice) ne nutně do md — patří jen pokud mění herní pocit nebo design intent.
67	- Před commitem zkontroluj že md a kód jsou konzistentní (typicky: hodnoty v `balance.json`, klávesy, damage hodnoty).
68	- Glamdring **není git repo** (zatím) — uživatel commituje ručně nebo si ho založí.
69	
70	## Out of scope (v1)
71	
72	- Touch input pro mobil.
73	- Další typy nepřátel nad rámec `enemy_basic` + `enemy_weak` (capability hooky připravené).
74	- Player stats / smrt (player invincible) — run končí jen ručním restartem (`Space`), žádná win/lose obrazovka.
75	- Zvuky, hudba.
76	- Reálný skeleton art (`skeleton-basic.png` je placeholder kopie `enemy_basic.png`).
77	- Sword sprity / AnimationPlayer (modulární API to umožní bez refaktoru).
78	- Persistence skóre, save game, menu / scene flow.

lastPrompt: asi budu chtit nejaky ingame obchod kde pujodu kupovat ingame upgrady za ingame menu ktera se bude vydelavat. dej navrh co byt mohlo byt co budou kupovat za upgrady

content.content:
1	extends Node2D
2	
3	# Fight scene controller. Owns the 16 Hz tick accumulator, calls combatant
4	# ticks in order, resolves swing events, updates the HUD, advances endless waves.
5	
6	const TICKS_PER_SECOND := 16
7	const TICK_DURATION := 1.0 / TICKS_PER_SECOND
8	
9	# Gameplay resolve numbers (damage, fatigue, regen, hit flash) live in
10	# `balance.json` and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual
11	# polish constants (shake/particles) remain as `const` below.
12	
13	# Polish fx — purely visual, no gameplay impact.
14	const SHAKE_HIT_WEAK := 4.0
15	const SHAKE_HIT_STRONG := 10.0
16	const SHAKE_FATIGUE := 3.0
17	const SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC := 30.0
18	const PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE := 10
19	const PARTICLES_FATIGUE := 6
20	const PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS := 0.45
21	const PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN := 80.0
22	const PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX := 220.0
23	const PARTICLE_SIZE := 4.0
24	const PARTICLE_DRAG := 0.92  # velocity multiplier per frame at 60 fps reference
25	const COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE := Color(1.0, 0.5, 0.3, 1.0)
26	const COLOR_FATIGUE_FX := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
27	const COLOR_PLAYER_HIT := Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.3, 1.0)
28	const COLOR_STAGGER_FX := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
29	const COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX := Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
30	const COLOR_CHIP := Color(0.7, 0.75, 0.85, 1.0)
31	const PARTICLES_CHIP := 4
32	
33	# Endless mode — enemy se spawnuje dynamicky podle wave composition.
34	const ENEMY_BASIC := preload("res://enemy_basic.gd")
35	const ENEMY_WEAK := preload("res://enemy_weak.gd")
36	const ENEMY_SPAWN_POS := Vector2(456, 384)
37	
38	@onready var player: Node2D = $Player
39	var enemy: Node2D
40	@onready var hud: CanvasLayer = $HUD
41	@onready var background_canvas: CanvasLayer = $Background
42	
43	var tick_acc: float = 0.0
44	
45	# Endless run state.
46	var wave: int = 0
47	var score: int = 0
48	var banner_ticks: int = 0
49	
50	# Hit-stop freeze timer (real seconds). While > 0 the tick loop is frozen but
51	# shake/particles/redraw keep updating — pure presentation, gameplay-neutral.
52	var hitstop_timer: float = 0.0
53	
54	# Polish fx state.
55	var shake_amount: float = 0.0
56	var rng_fx := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
57	# Each particle: {pos: Vector2, vel: Vector2, life: float, color: Color}
58	var particles: Array = []
59	
60	
61	func _ready() -> void:
62		_advance_wave()  # spawns wave 1 + banner
63	
64	
65	func _advance_wave() -> void:
66		wave += 1
67		_spawn_enemy(wave)
68		banner_ticks = Balance.endless_banner_ticks
69	
70	
71	func _spawn_enemy(w: int) -> void:
72		# composition: 1-2 weak, 3-4 basic, 5+ alternate (odd basic / even weak)
73		var script: GDScript
74		if w <= 2:
75			script = ENEMY_WEAK
76		elif w <= 4:
77			script = ENEMY_BASIC
78		else:
79			script = ENEMY_BASIC if (w % 2 == 1) else ENEMY_WEAK
80		var e: Node2D = script.new()
81		e.position = ENEMY_SPAWN_POS
82		if w >= 5:
83			# Mild scaling — applies only to basic (weak ignores speed_scale).
84			e.speed_scale = clampf(1.0 + float(w - 4) * Balance.endless_scale_step, 1.0, Balance.endless_scale_cap)
85			e.strong_prob_bonus = minf(float(w - 4) * 0.01, Balance.endless_strong_prob_bonus_cap)
86		if enemy != null and is_instance_valid(enemy):
87			enemy.queue_free()
88		add_child(e)
89		enemy = e
90	
91	
92	func _on_enemy_defeated() -> void:
93		score += Balance.endless_kill_score_per_wave * wave
94		_advance_wave()
95	
96	
97	func _process(delta: float) -> void:
98		if hitstop_timer > 0.0:
99			hitstop_timer = maxf(0.0, hitstop_timer - delta)
100		else:
101			tick_acc += delta
102			while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION and hitstop_timer <= 0.0:
103				tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
104				_on_tick()
105		_update_shake(delta)
106		_update_particles(delta)
107		queue_redraw()
108	
109	
110	func _update_shake(delta: float) -> void:
111		if shake_amount <= 0.0:
112			position = Vector2.ZERO
113			background_canvas.offset = Vector2.ZERO
114			return
115		var dir := Vector2(
116			rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0),
117			rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
118		)
119		if dir.length() > 0.001:
120			dir = dir.normalized()
121		position = dir * shake_amount
122		# Mirror shake into the background CanvasLayer (it's outside the Fight
123		# Node2D transform, so offset is the only way to shake it).
124		background_canvas.offset = position
125		shake_amount = maxf(0.0, shake_amount - SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC * delta)
126	
127	
128	func _update_particles(delta: float) -> void:
129		for i in range(particles.size() - 1, -1, -1):
130			var p: Dictionary = particles[i]
131			p.life -= delta
132			if p.life <= 0.0:
133				particles.remove_at(i)
134				continue
135			p.pos += p.vel * delta
136			p.vel *= PARTICLE_DRAG
137	
138	
139	func _trigger_shake(amount: float) -> void:
140		# Combine — don't downgrade an already-bigger shake in progress.
141		shake_amount = maxf(shake_amount, amount)
142	
143	
144	func _trigger_hitstop(amount: float) -> void:
145		# Combine — don't shorten an already-longer freeze in progress.
146		hitstop_timer = maxf(hitstop_timer, amount)
147	
148	
149	func _spawn_particles(at: Vector2, color: Color, count: int) -> void:
150		for _i in count:
151			var angle := rng_fx.randf_range(-PI, PI)
152			var speed := rng_fx.randf_range(PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN, PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX)
153			var vel := Vector2(cos(angle), sin(angle)) * speed
154			particles.append({
155				"pos": at,
156				"vel": vel,
157				"life": PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS,
158				"color": color,
159			})
160	
161	
162	func _between_combatants() -> Vector2:
163		return (player.position + enemy.position) * 0.5
164	
165	
166	func _draw() -> void:
167		for p: Dictionary in particles:
168			var alpha := clampf(p.life / PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS, 0.0, 1.0)
169			var col: Color = p.color
170			col.a = alpha
171			var s := PARTICLE_SIZE
172			draw_rect(Rect2(p.pos - Vector2(s, s) * 0.5, Vector2(s, s)), col)
173	
174	
175	func _on_tick() -> void:
176		# Space restarts the entire run at any time.
177		if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):
178			get_tree().reload_current_scene()
179			return
180		if banner_ticks > 0:
181			banner_ticks -= 1
182	
183		# Tick order: player first, enemy second. (Doesn't materially matter because
184		# resolution happens after both have ticked, but keeps deterministic.)
185		player.tick()
186		enemy.tick()
187	
188		# Player input handled at tick boundary (post-countdown so freshly-finished
189		# states allow new input on the same tick).
190		player.handle_input()
191	
192		# Resolve enemy swing first (enemy attack lands on player).
193		if enemy.swing_this_tick:
194			_resolve_enemy_swing()
195	
196		# Then player swing (player attack lands on enemy).
197		if player.swing_this_tick:
198			_resolve_player_swing()
199	
200		# Fatigue → exhausted transition (jen pro typy s fatigue — weak ji nemá).
201		if enemy.has_fatigue() and enemy.fatigue <= 0 and enemy.state != &"exhausted":
202			enemy.enter_exhausted()
203	
204		# Kill check → wave advance (must stay after both resolves so fx in this
205		# tick still reference the dying enemy's position).
206		if enemy.hp <= 0:
207			_on_enemy_defeated()
208	
209		_update_hud()
210	
211	
212	# --- Resolves ------------------------------------------------------------
213	
214	func _resolve_enemy_swing() -> void:
215		# Enemy swing event on the last tick of threat_*.
216		var kind: StringName = enemy.pending_attack_kind
217	
218		# 1. Player dodging (invincible window) → miss.
219		if player.state == &"dodging" and player.dodge_invincible:
220			return
221	
222		# 2. Weak attack + player blocking → block. Active parry drains enemy fatigue
223		# AND arms a riposte on the player. Perfect parry (block started within
224		# perfect_parry_ticks) additionally staggers the enemy. Passive block (held
225		# too long) just protects: no drain, no riposte.
226		if kind == &"weak" and player.state == &"blocking":
227			if player.is_active_block():
228				if enemy.has_fatigue():
229					enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
230				player.set_riposte_ready()
231				if player.is_perfect_parry():
232					enemy.enter_stagger()
233					_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
234					_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_stagger)
235					_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_STAGGER_FX, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
236				else:
237					_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
238					_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_parry)
239					_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
240			return
241	
242		# 3. Strong attack + player blocking → block ineffective (fall through).
243		# 4. Otherwise → player hit (player invincible, but enemy regens —
244		#    fatigue-less types get nothing, a miss just wastes the turn).
245		if enemy.has_fatigue():
246			enemy.heal_hp(Balance.enemy_hp_regen_on_hit)
247			enemy.add_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit)
248		_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_WEAK if kind == &"weak" else SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
249		player.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
250		_spawn_particles(player.position, COLOR_PLAYER_HIT, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
251		# Player got hit: break combo and a small hit-stop punch.
252		player.reset_combo()
253		_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)
254	
255	
256	func _resolve_player_swing() -> void:
257		var kind: StringName = player.pending_attack_kind
258		var es: StringName = enemy.state
259	
260		# Enemy in threat_* → no effect (telegraph cannot be interrupted).
261		if es == &"threat_weak" or es == &"threat_strong":
262			return
263	
264		# Enemy in idle → blocks player attacks, with strong draining fatigue.
265		# Fatigue-less types (weak skeleton) instead take reduced chip damage.
266		if es == &"idle":
267			var chip: float = enemy.idle_chip_multiplier()
268			if chip > 0.0:
269				# Chip-through-block: reduced damage straight to HP. No combo, no
270				# riposte consume, no execute — clean punishes stay the rewarded path.
271				var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
272				var dmg: int = int(round(float(base_dmg) * chip))
273				enemy.damage_hp(dmg)
274				score += dmg
275				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
276				_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)
277				_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_CHIP, PARTICLES_CHIP)
278				return
279			if kind == &"strong":
280				if enemy.has_fatigue():
281					enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle)
282				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
283				_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
284			# TODO: future visual block feedback could be a temporary sprite swap
285			# (e.g. a `block_react` stance cell) on enemy.gd. For now no extra fx.
286			return
287	
288		# Enemy in recovery_*, exhausted, or staggered → hit lands. Riposte and
289		# execute multipliers stack multiplicatively; round once at the end.
290		if es == &"recovery_weak" or es == &"recovery_strong" or es == &"exhausted" or es == &"staggered":
291			var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
292			var dmg: float = float(base_dmg)
293			var is_riposte: bool = player.is_riposte_ready()
294			if is_riposte:
295				dmg *= Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier
296			var is_execute: bool = enemy.hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp
297			if is_execute:
298				dmg *= Balance.execute_damage_multiplier
299			enemy.damage_hp(int(round(dmg)))
300			score += int(round(dmg))
301			if is_riposte:
302				player.consume_riposte()
303			player.add_combo()
304			# juice
305			var big: bool = is_riposte or is_execute or kind == &"strong"
306			_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG if big else SHAKE_HIT_WEAK)
307			_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_strong if big else Balance.hitstop_weak)
308			enemy.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
309			var fx_col: Color = COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX if is_riposte else COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE
310			_spawn_particles(enemy.position, fx_col, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
311			return
312	
313		# Fallback (shouldn't normally hit): treat as blocked.
314	
315	
316	func _update_hud() -> void:
317		# State-ticks field is repurposed per-state for visibility:
318		#   blocking → active parry window remaining (0..Balance.block_active_ticks)
319		#   other    → state_ticks_remaining
320		var p_ticks: int = player.state_ticks_remaining
321		if player.state == &"blocking":
322			p_ticks = player.active_block_ticks_remaining()
323		hud.update(
324			enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
325			player.state, p_ticks, player.dodge_cooldown_ticks,
326			player.combo, player.combo_rank(), player.max_combo,
327			wave, score, banner_ticks
328		)

file.content:
extends Node2D

# Fight scene controller. Owns the 16 Hz tick accumulator, calls combatant
# ticks in order, resolves swing events, updates the HUD, advances endless waves.

const TICKS_PER_SECOND := 16
const TICK_DURATION := 1.0 / TICKS_PER_SECOND

# Gameplay resolve numbers (damage, fatigue, regen, hit flash) live in
# `balance.json` and are accessed via the `Balance` autoload. Only visual
# polish constants (shake/particles) remain as `const` below.

# Polish fx — purely visual, no gameplay impact.
const SHAKE_HIT_WEAK := 4.0
const SHAKE_HIT_STRONG := 10.0
const SHAKE_FATIGUE := 3.0
const SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC := 30.0
const PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE := 10
const PARTICLES_FATIGUE := 6
const PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS := 0.45
const PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN := 80.0
const PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX := 220.0
const PARTICLE_SIZE := 4.0
const PARTICLE_DRAG := 0.92  # velocity multiplier per frame at 60 fps reference
const COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE := Color(1.0, 0.5, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_FATIGUE_FX := Color(0.95, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_PLAYER_HIT := Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_STAGGER_FX := Color(0.5, 0.85, 1.0, 1.0)
const COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX := Color(1.0, 0.85, 0.3, 1.0)
const COLOR_CHIP := Color(0.7, 0.75, 0.85, 1.0)
const PARTICLES_CHIP := 4

# Endless mode — enemy se spawnuje dynamicky podle wave composition.
const ENEMY_BASIC := preload("res://enemy_basic.gd")
const ENEMY_WEAK := preload("res://enemy_weak.gd")
const ENEMY_SPAWN_POS := Vector2(456, 384)

@onready var player: Node2D = $Player
var enemy: Node2D
@onready var hud: CanvasLayer = $HUD
@onready var background_canvas: CanvasLayer = $Background

var tick_acc: float = 0.0

# Endless run state.
var wave: int = 0
var score: int = 0
var banner_ticks: int = 0

# Hit-stop freeze timer (real seconds). While > 0 the tick loop is frozen but
# shake/particles/redraw keep updating — pure presentation, gameplay-neutral.
var hitstop_timer: float = 0.0

# Polish fx state.
var shake_amount: float = 0.0
var rng_fx := RandomNumberGenerator.new()
# Each particle: {pos: Vector2, vel: Vector2, life: float, color: Color}
var particles: Array = []


func _ready() -> void:
	_advance_wave()  # spawns wave 1 + banner


func _advance_wave() -> void:
	wave += 1
	_spawn_enemy(wave)
	banner_ticks = Balance.endless_banner_ticks


func _spawn_enemy(w: int) -> void:
	# composition: 1-2 weak, 3-4 basic, 5+ alternate (odd basic / even weak)
	var script: GDScript
	if w <= 2:
		script = ENEMY_WEAK
	elif w <= 4:
		script = ENEMY_BASIC
	else:
		script = ENEMY_BASIC if (w % 2 == 1) else ENEMY_WEAK
	var e: Node2D = script.new()
	e.position = ENEMY_SPAWN_POS
	if w >= 5:
		# Mild scaling — applies only to basic (weak ignores speed_scale).
		e.speed_scale = clampf(1.0 + float(w - 4) * Balance.endless_scale_step, 1.0, Balance.endless_scale_cap)
		e.strong_prob_bonus = minf(float(w - 4) * 0.01, Balance.endless_strong_prob_bonus_cap)
	if enemy != null and is_instance_valid(enemy):
		enemy.queue_free()
	add_child(e)
	enemy = e


func _on_enemy_defeated() -> void:
	score += Balance.endless_kill_score_per_wave * wave
	_advance_wave()


func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	if hitstop_timer > 0.0:
		hitstop_timer = maxf(0.0, hitstop_timer - delta)
	else:
		tick_acc += delta
		while tick_acc >= TICK_DURATION and hitstop_timer <= 0.0:
			tick_acc -= TICK_DURATION
			_on_tick()
	_update_shake(delta)
	_update_particles(delta)
	queue_redraw()


func _update_shake(delta: float) -> void:
	if shake_amount <= 0.0:
		position = Vector2.ZERO
		background_canvas.offset = Vector2.ZERO
		return
	var dir := Vector2(
		rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0),
		rng_fx.randf_range(-1.0, 1.0)
	)
	if dir.length() > 0.001:
		dir = dir.normalized()
	position = dir * shake_amount
	# Mirror shake into the background CanvasLayer (it's outside the Fight
	# Node2D transform, so offset is the only way to shake it).
	background_canvas.offset = position
	shake_amount = maxf(0.0, shake_amount - SHAKE_DECAY_PER_SEC * delta)


func _update_particles(delta: float) -> void:
	for i in range(particles.size() - 1, -1, -1):
		var p: Dictionary = particles[i]
		p.life -= delta
		if p.life <= 0.0:
			particles.remove_at(i)
			continue
		p.pos += p.vel * delta
		p.vel *= PARTICLE_DRAG


func _trigger_shake(amount: float) -> void:
	# Combine — don't downgrade an already-bigger shake in progress.
	shake_amount = maxf(shake_amount, amount)


func _trigger_hitstop(amount: float) -> void:
	# Combine — don't shorten an already-longer freeze in progress.
	hitstop_timer = maxf(hitstop_timer, amount)


func _spawn_particles(at: Vector2, color: Color, count: int) -> void:
	for _i in count:
		var angle := rng_fx.randf_range(-PI, PI)
		var speed := rng_fx.randf_range(PARTICLE_SPEED_MIN, PARTICLE_SPEED_MAX)
		var vel := Vector2(cos(angle), sin(angle)) * speed
		particles.append({
			"pos": at,
			"vel": vel,
			"life": PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS,
			"color": color,
		})


func _between_combatants() -> Vector2:
	return (player.position + enemy.position) * 0.5


func _draw() -> void:
	for p: Dictionary in particles:
		var alpha := clampf(p.life / PARTICLE_LIFE_SECONDS, 0.0, 1.0)
		var col: Color = p.color
		col.a = alpha
		var s := PARTICLE_SIZE
		draw_rect(Rect2(p.pos - Vector2(s, s) * 0.5, Vector2(s, s)), col)


func _on_tick() -> void:
	# Space restarts the entire run at any time.
	if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):
		get_tree().reload_current_scene()
		return
	if banner_ticks > 0:
		banner_ticks -= 1

	# Tick order: player first, enemy second. (Doesn't materially matter because
	# resolution happens after both have ticked, but keeps deterministic.)
	player.tick()
	enemy.tick()

	# Player input handled at tick boundary (post-countdown so freshly-finished
	# states allow new input on the same tick).
	player.handle_input()

	# Resolve enemy swing first (enemy attack lands on player).
	if enemy.swing_this_tick:
		_resolve_enemy_swing()

	# Then player swing (player attack lands on enemy).
	if player.swing_this_tick:
		_resolve_player_swing()

	# Fatigue → exhausted transition (jen pro typy s fatigue — weak ji nemá).
	if enemy.has_fatigue() and enemy.fatigue <= 0 and enemy.state != &"exhausted":
		enemy.enter_exhausted()

	# Kill check → wave advance (must stay after both resolves so fx in this
	# tick still reference the dying enemy's position).
	if enemy.hp <= 0:
		_on_enemy_defeated()

	_update_hud()


# --- Resolves ------------------------------------------------------------

func _resolve_enemy_swing() -> void:
	# Enemy swing event on the last tick of threat_*.
	var kind: StringName = enemy.pending_attack_kind

	# 1. Player dodging (invincible window) → miss.
	if player.state == &"dodging" and player.dodge_invincible:
		return

	# 2. Weak attack + player blocking → block. Active parry drains enemy fatigue
	# AND arms a riposte on the player. Perfect parry (block started within
	# perfect_parry_ticks) additionally staggers the enemy. Passive block (held
	# too long) just protects: no drain, no riposte.
	if kind == &"weak" and player.state == &"blocking":
		if player.is_active_block():
			if enemy.has_fatigue():
				enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_blocked_weak)
			player.set_riposte_ready()
			if player.is_perfect_parry():
				enemy.enter_stagger()
				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
				_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_stagger)
				_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_STAGGER_FX, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
			else:
				_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
				_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_parry)
				_spawn_particles(_between_combatants(), COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
		return

	# 3. Strong attack + player blocking → block ineffective (fall through).
	# 4. Otherwise → player hit (player invincible, but enemy regens —
	#    fatigue-less types get nothing, a miss just wastes the turn).
	if enemy.has_fatigue():
		enemy.heal_hp(Balance.enemy_hp_regen_on_hit)
		enemy.add_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_regen_on_hit)
	_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_WEAK if kind == &"weak" else SHAKE_HIT_STRONG)
	player.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
	_spawn_particles(player.position, COLOR_PLAYER_HIT, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
	# Player got hit: break combo and a small hit-stop punch.
	player.reset_combo()
	_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)


func _resolve_player_swing() -> void:
	var kind: StringName = player.pending_attack_kind
	var es: StringName = enemy.state

	# Enemy in threat_* → no effect (telegraph cannot be interrupted).
	if es == &"threat_weak" or es == &"threat_strong":
		return

	# Enemy in idle → blocks player attacks, with strong draining fatigue.
	# Fatigue-less types (weak skeleton) instead take reduced chip damage.
	if es == &"idle":
		var chip: float = enemy.idle_chip_multiplier()
		if chip > 0.0:
			# Chip-through-block: reduced damage straight to HP. No combo, no
			# riposte consume, no execute — clean punishes stay the rewarded path.
			var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
			var dmg: int = int(round(float(base_dmg) * chip))
			enemy.damage_hp(dmg)
			score += dmg
			_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
			_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_weak)
			_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_CHIP, PARTICLES_CHIP)
			return
		if kind == &"strong":
			if enemy.has_fatigue():
				enemy.drain_fatigue(Balance.enemy_fatigue_player_strong_idle)
			_trigger_shake(SHAKE_FATIGUE)
			_spawn_particles(enemy.position, COLOR_FATIGUE_FX, PARTICLES_FATIGUE)
		# TODO: future visual block feedback could be a temporary sprite swap
		# (e.g. a `block_react` stance cell) on enemy.gd. For now no extra fx.
		return

	# Enemy in recovery_*, exhausted, or staggered → hit lands. Riposte and
	# execute multipliers stack multiplicatively; round once at the end.
	if es == &"recovery_weak" or es == &"recovery_strong" or es == &"exhausted" or es == &"staggered":
		var base_dmg: int = Balance.hp_damage_weak if kind == &"weak" else Balance.hp_damage_strong
		var dmg: float = float(base_dmg)
		var is_riposte: bool = player.is_riposte_ready()
		if is_riposte:
			dmg *= Balance.riposte_damage_multiplier
		var is_execute: bool = enemy.hp <= Balance.execute_threshold_hp
		if is_execute:
			dmg *= Balance.execute_damage_multiplier
		enemy.damage_hp(int(round(dmg)))
		score += int(round(dmg))
		if is_riposte:
			player.consume_riposte()
		player.add_combo()
		# juice
		var big: bool = is_riposte or is_execute or kind == &"strong"
		_trigger_shake(SHAKE_HIT_STRONG if big else SHAKE_HIT_WEAK)
		_trigger_hitstop(Balance.hitstop_strong if big else Balance.hitstop_weak)
		enemy.trigger_flash(Balance.hit_flash_ticks)
		var fx_col: Color = COLOR_RIPOSTE_FX if is_riposte else COLOR_HIT_DAMAGE
		_spawn_particles(enemy.position, fx_col, PARTICLES_HIT_DAMAGE)
		return

	# Fallback (shouldn't normally hit): treat as blocked.


func _update_hud() -> void:
	# State-ticks field is repurposed per-state for visibility:
	#   blocking → active parry window remaining (0..Balance.block_active_ticks)
	#   other    → state_ticks_remaining
	var p_ticks: int = player.state_ticks_remaining
	if player.state == &"blocking":
		p_ticks = player.active_block_ticks_remaining()
	hud.update(
		enemy.hp, enemy.fatigue, enemy.state, enemy.state_ticks_remaining,
		player.state, p_ticks, player.dodge_cooldown_ticks,
		player.combo, player.combo_rank(), player.max_combo,
		wave, score, banner_ticks
	)

1	extends Enemy
2	
3	# Weak skeleton: fatigue-less chip enemy. Nemá fatigue (žádný exhausted, žádný
4	# regen když zasáhne playera), zato player hity v jeho idle "chipují" skrz
5	# block za redukovaný damage (chip_multiplier). Low HP, dlouhé čitelné
6	# telegraphy, generous recovery, skoro jen weak útoky, žádné feinty.
7	# Per-type tunables žijí v `balance.json` sekci `enemy_weak`, čtené přes
8	# `Balance.enemy_weak` Dictionary (záměrně neflattenované — kolize s
9	# enemy_basic flat vars).
10	
11	const BODY_SHEET := preload("res://sprites/skeleton-basic.png")     # placeholder copy of enemy_basic.png — real skeleton art TODO
12	const WEAPON_SHEET_WEAK := preload("res://sprites/enemy_basic_weapon.png")  # reuse — skeleton weapon art TODO
13	const BASE_TINT := Color(0.92, 0.95, 0.82, 1.0)   # bony pale tint to tell it apart from basic until real art

15	
16	func _sprite_sheet() -> Texture2D:

19

23

26	
27

29		return false
30	
31	
32	func idle_chip_multiplier() -> float:
33		return float(Balance.enemy_weak.get("chip_multiplier", 0.34))
34	
35

39

41		return &"strong" if rng_.randf() < float(Balance.enemy_weak.get("strong_probability", 0.05)) else &"weak"
42

72	
73
